What’s New
Keep up with the latest releases, improvements, and fixes.
Follow @NotionHQ
↗
July 1, 2026
3.6
Notion 3.6: External Agents, HTML blocks, and more
Your agents shouldn’t be stuck in one-off chats. Now you can bring multiple agents into a single UI with the rest of your team, so you can automate entire workflows end-to-end.
And there’s a lot more. AI Meeting Notes now include
speaker labels
(a top request!), your Notion Agents can read and write more filetypes like
Excel
and
PowerPoint
, and you can build
interactive HTML blocks,
like an ROI calculator.
Orchestrate External Agents (Claude, Cursor, and more soon)
Some of your agents might live in CLIs, IDEs, and separate apps, doing solo work the rest of the team can’t see or build on. Now you can assign them tasks from a board shared with your whole team, @-mention them like teammates, and watch them run.
Claude and Cursor are the first two External Agents in Notion
. That means you can reimagine entire end-to-end processes with full automation, right alongside the rest of your team’s work.
Get started →
Smarter meeting summaries with speaker labels
AI Meeting Notes now identifies speakers in your meetings
, based on whose microphone is active. This improves meeting summaries and makes sure follow-ups are assigned to the right teammate. And this is just the beginning; we’ll keep making speaker identification even better!
Download the desktop app for the best experience →
Add interactive HTML blocks to your Notion docs
Notion Agents can now create interactive
HTML blocks,
and we can’t stop tinkering with them. The best part is that they live in Notion, so anyone on your team can use them or iterate on them. Some examples we love:
Build a ROI calculator:
Watch your customers’ eyes light up when they see how much they’ll save by using your solution. Customize it with details like their industry or current tool stack.
Quiz your teammates:
Turn a doc about your latest feature into an interactive quiz your team will remember. Your Agent will identify what matters, create relevant questions, and score results at the end.
Map your org chart:
Visualize your people database as an org chart so new hires onboard faster and cross-functional work gets done. Do it directly in Notion, with no need to open another tool.
Try it out →
Your Notion Agents now speak Microsoft
Your Notion Agents now work with
PPTX, XLSX, DOCX,
and more. Give your agents
context
by attaching spreadsheets, decks, Word docs, and PDFs—your agent reads them like any other source.
And your agents can
create
these files for you too: turn a page into a polished PowerPoint, spin a database into an Excel model, or summarize a pile of PDFs into one clean doc. Your legal and finance teams will thank you. Notion 🤝 Microsoft.
Your agent can use Outlook for you
Another nice one for our Microsoft shops. Connect
Outlook Mail and Calendar
and your Notion Agents can run your inbox and your schedule for you. Triage incoming email, draft replies in your voice, and send messages; all you have to do is ask.
Your agent can find open times, schedule meetings, and keep your calendar in order so you spend less time wrangling logistics and more time on the work that matters.
Five new MCP connections for Custom Agents
People are loving Notion MCP—usage has 10×’d in the past month. Now your Custom Agents get five more connections out of the box:
Mercury, Mixpanel, Miro, Box, and ClickHouse
.
They join the connections already available (like Figma, GitHub, Zapier), so your agents can pull financial data from Mercury, dig into product analytics in Mixpanel, or update a Miro board, all with no custom setup required. Find and connect every available integration from the new connections tab in your settings.
Learn more →
More models, without the lock-in
Your agents support a fresh lineup of new models, so you can pick the right brain for the job, whether you need speed, deeper reasoning, or a creative spark. Use the latest frontier intelligence from
Opus 4.8
or
Grok 4.3
, or choose a more cost-efficient model like the new open-weight
GLM 5.2
.
Track Custom Agent actions in the audit log
Your security team now gets more visibility into how AI is being used. The
audit log
includes Custom Agent activity, so you can see when an agent runs, what it changed, and who triggered it, making it easier to review workflows, troubleshoot issues, and stay compliant. Available on Enterprise.
And a few more …
Custom Agents for Slack Enterprise Grid
: Custom Agents now work across your full Slack org. Talk to your sales rep or
learn more
.
Notion Workers in the credit dashboard:
Find Worker usage in the Notion credits dashboard, so admins can track everything in one place.
Learn more →
Build Notion Workers on Windows:
Windows developers, you’re in. The Notion CLI now installs on Windows through npm, so building and deploying Notion Workers is no longer limited to Mac and Linux.
Read the docs →
Upload audio to AI Meeting Notes:
Bring meetings recorded elsewhere into Notion. Upload an audio file and AI Meeting Notes will transcribe and summarize it like any other meeting.
Duplicate custom agents:
Found a setup that works? Copy any custom agent in a click and tweak it for a new team or use case—no need to rebuild from scratch.
Agents can use your people directory:
Now you can give your agents access to your people directory, so they have more context about your team.
Learn more →
May 26, 2026
Merge cells in simple tables
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
Finally! Merge cells in simple tables.
Create headers that span multiple columns or group related rows into structured layouts.
Give it a try: select multiple cells → open the cell menu → select
Merge cells
.
See the
announcement
on X →
May 13, 2026
3.5
3.5: Notion Developer Platform
Notion? For developers? Fair question. It’s true, we haven’t always been the most developer-focused platform. Today that changes.
Introducing: the
Notion Developer Platform.
Now you (and your coding agents) can write code to sync any data and build any agent tool, all running on our infrastructure. You can also bring your favorite agents into Notion (like Claude, Codex, Decagon, or ones you’ve built yourself). It turns Notion into one shared canvas with all your data, for your team and agents to work together.
Learn more →
Orchestrate all your team’s agents (Alpha)
Bring your favorite agents into Notion with the External Agents API, even the ones you built yourself. We’ve also partnered with Claude, Codex, Decagon, and more so they work out of the box. Now Notion is your orchestration layer: a Decagon ticket routes to your coding agent, which proposes a fix and loops in your team to approve. Anyone can work with agents in Notion, not just engineers.
Join the External Agents waitlist →
Notion is our AI layer because it’s where work is created or imagined—and we want our agents as close to the action as possible.
Dan Gilbert
CEO at Brainlabs
Sync
any
data source (Beta)
Sync any data source with an API into your Notion databases. No servers for your team to manage. Our new database sync is powered by Workers that run on our infrastructure (more on this below). Pull in tickets from Zendesk so agents can take a first pass on the fix. Sync customer data from Salesforce for agents to build detailed reports. Connect Strava and Spotify data to curate the perfect running playlist. Whatever context you need can now live in Notion.
Watch the demo →
Workers give us the tools to build deep integrations into Notion that simply couldn't exist before. We have a worker that runs every night that syncs and converts uneditable PDFs in our Google Drive into rich, fully editable pages in an organized Notion database. It unlocks this data for our team and agents - and saves us tons of time.
Sam Lambert
CEO at PlanetScale
Build custom tools for your agents (Beta)
Give your Custom Agents capabilities that Notion and MCP don’t cover on their own. Write your logic in code and deploy it as a Worker. It’s
deterministic
, so it’s more reliable than LLM reasoning, and a
fraction of the token cost
. Use them to generate assets, query internal data, or take action in any other app.
Read the docs →
I think of Notion Workers as infrastructure: they auto-populate, auto-update, and set up the systems I need.
Austin Tedesco
Head of Growth at Every
Trigger Notion workflows from anywhere (Beta)
Webhooks used to be a one-way street: Notion could trigger your other apps, but not the other way around. Now any app can trigger Notion directly. A Worker receives the webhook, runs your logic, and takes action in Notion or calls other APIs. Use it to close tasks when a PR merges, update your CRM when a subscription changes, or create an onboarding doc when an offer is signed.
Read the docs →
Meet your Notion Workers
Database sync, agent tools, and webhook triggers are all powered by a new primitive we’re calling Workers. Notion Workers are our hosted runtime for custom code, so you can extend Notion without running your own servers. You and your coding agent write the code, deploy it through the CLI, and run it in a secure sandbox. Workers are free to try during the beta period. Starting August 11 2026, Workers will run on Notion credits.
Read the docs →
Workers let us connect directly to other tools’ APIs and automate what used to be manual handoffs. Notion becomes the connective layer, and Workers fill in whatever gaps exist between your tools.
Brian Emerick
Technical Program Manager at Vercel
A Notion CLI, built for devs and coding agents
The Notion command-line interface (CLI), made specifically for developers and coding agents, is a new way to work with Notion programmatically. Use it to sign in to your workspace, read and take action in Notion, build and deploy Workers, and extend Notion however your team needs. To install, run curl -fsSL https://ntn.dev | bash.
Watch the demo →
Use your Notion Agents in any app (Alpha)
Soon, your Notion Agents won’t have to stay in Notion. With the Notion Agent SDK, you can embed an agent inside your other tools. Trigger a deal report from a button in your CRM. Answer repeat questions inside MS Teams or Discord with verified knowledge from your workspace. Or pull customer context into Amplitude, Hex, or any dashboard.
Join the Agent SDK waitlist →
Manage all connections from one tab
We’ve updated the Connections tab in workspace settings. Now, every connection lives in one place, so your team can see everything that’s available at a glance. It includes personal and workspace connections, personal access tokens for API authentication, and internal API connections. And each app shows every connection type in one listing. Go to
Settings
→
Connections
to check it out (or
click here
).
Agents “hall of fame”
Knowing what agents to build can be the hardest part, so we pulled together the best agents from companies like Ramp, Clay, and Vercel into one library. Each one comes with a checklist of exactly what you need (databases, pages, tools) and a starter prompt to copy/paste. Pick one and set it up in minutes.
Browse the collection →
More updates your devs will love
Markdown API:
ICYMI read and write Notion pages as Markdown. Built for the way agents already think.
Notion MCP:
Now works with Meeting Notes and block comments, plus creating and updating databases are 91% more token-efficient.
Notion API:
Any member can build connections (not just Workspace Owners). Plus workspace-scoped OAuth and personal access tokens.
See releases →
Developer Portal:
app.notion.com/developers
is now a dedicated portal for creating, managing, and listing your connections and tokens.
Developer Docs:
Rebuilt and streamlined for clarity with a built-in AI assistant to help you find what you need.
This is just the beginning for the
Notion Developer Platform
. Any data, any tool, any agent, all running on our infrastructure. We can’t wait to see what you build.
Keep the feedback coming!
Ivan
P.S. We announced all of this and more at Make with Notion: Developer Platform.
Watch the keynote →
P.P.S. Curious what teams are already building? See how
Every
,
Brainlabs
, and
Vercel
are using our developer platform in production.
P.P.P.S. One more thing. You can now merge cells in simple tables, just like a spreadsheet. We’re excited about this one too.
May 7, 2026
Plan Mode
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
When you ask your agent to make significant changes like rewriting pages or updating databases in bulk, results can drift off course if your intent is unclear. Plan Mode introduces a preliminary step where your agent asks clarifying questions and builds a detailed plan before acting. The result is fewer surprises and more confidence in complex, multi-step work.
Learn more about
Plan Mode
from the Help Center →
May 6, 2026
New Custom Agent Directory
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
Custom Agents now have a dedicated place in your Library. Use the Custom Agent Directory to browse all your workspace’s agents, pin your favorites, and create new ones to automate your team’s busywork 24/7.
Get started from the sidebar:
Library
→
Agents
.
May 5, 2026

analysis.name: Notion
analysis.tagline: The AI workspace that works for you.
analysis.value_proposition: An AI-native workspace that unifies docs, knowledge base, projects, and autonomous agents so teams can capture context, find answers, and automate work end-to-end.
analysis.ideal_customer: Cross-functional teams (eng, product, design, marketing, IT) at startups through Fortune 100 that want a single system of record combined with AI agents that act on it

features.name: docs
features.description: Rich collaborative documents built from composable blocks

features.name: knowledge base
features.description: Central source of truth for teams and agents
features.evidence_quote: Knowledge Base. One source of truth for teams and agents.

features.name: projects
features.description: Project tracking with less overhead, more progress

features.name: databases
features.description: Organize projects/tasks with subtasks, dependencies, custom properties
features.evidence_quote: Databases including subtasks, dependencies, custom properties and more

features.name: database views
features.description: Multiple visualizations (table, board, calendar, timeline) of database data

features.name: sub-tasks and dependencies
features.description: Hierarchical task relations inside databases

features.name: charts
features.description: Visualize database data as donut, bar, and line charts
features.evidence_quote: Visualize data from databases in different ways (e.g. donut, bar, and line charts). Unlimited charts

features.name: forms
features.description: Basic and custom forms to capture responses into Notion
features.evidence_quote: Basic forms ... Custom forms ... Remove Notion branding from public forms

features.name: sites
features.description: Publish Notion pages as websites; custom themes, favicon, GA
features.evidence_quote: Publish a Notion page to the web ... Custom sites ... integrate with Google Analytics

features.name: web clipper
features.description: Browser extension to save web content into Notion

features.name: notion calendar
features.description: Standalone calendar app that connects multiple calendars
features.evidence_quote: Connect multiple Calendars to manage work and time together. Notion Calendar

features.name: notion mail
features.description: Email client integrated with Notion workspace

features.name: notion ai
features.description: Generative AI to write, summarize, and autofill databases
features.evidence_quote: Trial AI capabilities like generating docs, or autofilling databases

features.name: notion agent
features.description: Autonomous agent that completes multi-step tasks using workspace context
features.evidence_quote: Does work for you. Completes complex, multi-step tasks using context from Notion, your connected apps, and the web. Notion Agent

features.name: custom agents
features.description: User-built AI agents that automate repetitive team work
features.evidence_quote: Custom Agents. Automate repetitive work for your team.

features.name: external agents
features.description: Bring Claude, Cursor and other outside agents into Notion workflows
features.evidence_quote: Claude and Cursor are the first two External Agents in Notion

features.name: q&a agents
features.description: Agents that answer questions from existing workspace knowledge
features.evidence_quote: Q&A agents. Answers questions instantly using knowledge you already have.

features.name: task routing agents
features.description: Agents that assign, prioritize, and route tasks automatically
features.evidence_quote: Task routing agents. Assigns, prioritizes, and routes tasks on its own.

features.name: reporting agents
features.description: Agents that summarize, write, and send recurring reports
features.evidence_quote: Reporting agents. Summarizes, writes, and sends reports for you.

features.name: ai meeting notes
features.description: Automated meeting transcription, summary, and follow-ups
features.evidence_quote: Automate your meeting notes and follow-ups, no bot needed. AI Meeting Notes

features.name: speaker labels
features.description: AI Meeting Notes identifies speakers by active microphone
features.evidence_quote: AI Meeting Notes now identifies speakers in your meetings

features.name: audio upload for meeting notes
features.description: Upload recorded audio to be transcribed and summarized
features.evidence_quote: Upload an audio file and AI Meeting Notes will transcribe and summarize it

features.name: enterprise search
features.description: Unified search across Notion and connected apps like Slack and GitHub
features.evidence_quote: Search across connected apps like Slack, Github & more. Enterprise Search

features.name: citations in answers
features.description: AI answers include citations back to source pages

features.name: html blocks
features.description: Interactive HTML blocks (calculators, quizzes, org charts) generated by agents
features.evidence_quote: you can build interactive HTML blocks, like an ROI calculator.

features.name: verified pages
features.description: Mark pages up-to-date with a verified badge shown in search/AI citations
features.evidence_quote: Add a verified badge to pages that are up to date. Appears in search results and AI citations.

features.name: granular database permissions
features.description: Row-level access so collaborators only see assigned records
features.evidence_quote: Limit access to rows where the collaborator is assigned.

features.name: private teamspaces
features.description: Teamspaces hidden from non-members for sensitive work
features.evidence_quote: Create teamspaces that can't be seen or discovered by anyone except those you add.

features.name: guests vs members
features.description: Distinct guest role for external collaborators

features.name: sharing and permissions
features.description: Page/workspace-level sharing controls

features.name: comments and reminders
features.description: Inline comments and time-based reminders for collaboration
features.evidence_quote: Collaborate with others using comments and reminders.

features.name: page history
features.description: Restore pages to previous versions
features.evidence_quote: Restore your page to a previous version. Page history 7 days ... Unlimited

features.name: import and export
features.description: Bring data in and take it out of Notion

features.name: automations
features.description: Trigger workflows on database changes

features.name: connections
features.description: Native integrations with Slack, Google Drive, GitHub, and more
features.evidence_quote: Connect to some tools like Slack & Google Drive. Basic connections

features.name: premium connections
features.description: Higher-tier connections including GitHub and Asana
features.evidence_quote: Connect to GitHub, Asana & more. Premium connections

features.name: advanced connections
features.description: Enterprise-tier expanded integration catalog
features.evidence_quote: Connect to even more tools. Advanced connections

features.name: mcp connections
features.description: Model Context Protocol connectors for Custom Agents (Mercury, Mixpanel, Miro, Box, ClickHouse, Figma, GitHub, Zapier)
features.evidence_quote: Five new MCP connections for Custom Agents ... Mercury, Mixpanel, Miro, Box, and ClickHouse

features.name: notion workers
features.description: Custom code to build agent tools, sync external data, and trigger workflows
features.evidence_quote: Extend Notion with custom code to build agent tools, sync external data, and trigger Notion workflows

features.name: notion cli
features.description: Command-line tool to build and deploy Notion Workers (Mac, Linux, Windows via npm)
features.evidence_quote: The Notion CLI now installs on Windows through npm

features.name: public api
features.description: Developer API for building integrations

features.name: saml sso
features.description: Enterprise single sign-on via SAML
features.evidence_quote: Manage employee access at scale with secure single sign-on. SAML SSO

features.name: scim provisioning
features.description: User and group provisioning via SCIM API
features.evidence_quote: Access the Notion SCIM API to provision and manage users and groups. User provisioning (SCIM)

features.name: domain verification
features.description: Verify email domain ownership to unlock advanced security
features.evidence_quote: Verify ownership of an email domain to access advanced security features

features.name: domain management
features.description: Claim, transfer, or delete workspaces on a verified domain
features.evidence_quote: Establish ownership of your company domain and take actions on workspaces

features.name: audit log
features.description: Detailed log of security and workspace activity, including Custom Agent actions
features.evidence_quote: Access a detailed log of security and safety-related activity

features.name: advanced security and controls
features.description: Prevent external sharing, disable guests, workspace-level rules
features.evidence_quote: Unlock extra permission controls to prevent certain people from sharing pages externally

features.name: zero data retention
features.description: LLM providers apply zero data retention for Enterprise workspaces
features.evidence_quote: our LLM providers utilize zero data retention for Enterprise plan workspaces

features.name: dlp and siem connections
features.description: Integrate with SIEM and DLP tools for compliance

features.name: customer success manager
features.description: Dedicated CSM for Enterprise accounts

features.name: notion academy
features.description: Learning platform with badges and certifications
features.evidence_quote: learn Notion, build smarter workflows, and validate your skills with badges & certifications

features.name: templates marketplace
features.description: Large gallery of user- and Notion-made templates

features.name: consultants marketplace
features.description: Directory of certified Notion consultants

features.name: mobile apps
features.description: Native iOS and Android apps

features.name: desktop apps
features.description: Native Mac and Windows clients

features.name: multi-model support
features.description: Choose across frontier and open-weight models (Opus 4.8, Grok 4.3, GLM 5.2)
features.evidence_quote: Use the latest frontier intelligence from Opus 4.8 or Grok 4.3, or choose ... open-weight GLM 5.2

features.name: custom agents for slack enterprise grid
features.description: Custom Agents run across a full Slack org
features.evidence_quote: Custom Agents for Slack Enterprise Grid

features.name: people directory
features.description: Agents can query the org's people directory
features.evidence_quote: Agents can use your people directory

features.name: microsoft file support
features.description: Agents read/write PPTX, XLSX, DOCX, PDFs
features.evidence_quote: Your Notion Agents now work with PPTX, XLSX, DOCX, and more.

features.name: outlook integration
features.description: Agents triage Outlook Mail and schedule via Outlook Calendar
features.evidence_quote: Connect Outlook Mail and Calendar and your Notion Agents can run your inbox

differentiators: AI-native workspace combining docs, KB, projects with agents that act on the same data
External Agents: Claude and Cursor can be assigned tasks and @-mentioned like teammates
Interactive HTML blocks generated by agents (ROI calculators, quizzes, org charts)
Model choice across frontier and open-weight LLMs without lock-in
G2's #1 knowledge base for 3 consecutive years; 62% of Fortune 100 and 98% of Forbes Cloud 100 use Notion
Verified pages that flow into AI citations for trust
MCP-based extensibility with rapidly growing connector catalog

praised: 'Notion's thoughtful design speeds up collaboration and decisions' — Renee Solorzano
'Using the most AI-native tools like Notion is an important competitive advantage' — Michael Truell, Cursor
G2's #1 knowledge base for 3 consecutive years
HN interest in Notion as an all-in-one workspace for notes, tasks, wikis, databases
complaints: HN: 'Notion is withholding my company data, what can I do?' — data lock-in / export friction concerns
HN thread traction for open-source alternatives (Docs, Focalboard, Confluence/Notion clones) indicates desire for self-hostable substitutes
G2 review content was not accessible during scrape (page returned no content)

free_tier.limits: Unlimited pages for individuals but limited pages/blocks for 2+ members, 5MB file upload cap, 7-day page history, trial of Notion AI

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: Trial of Notion AI, basic forms/sites, Notion Calendar, databases w/ subtasks & dependencies

tiers.name: Plus
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited blocks/file uploads, custom forms/sites, unlimited charts, basic connections (Slack, Google Drive)

tiers.name: Business
tiers.headline_inclusions: Notion Agent, AI Meeting Notes, Enterprise Search (beta), SAML SSO, granular DB perms, private teamspaces, premium connections

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: SCIM, audit log, advanced security, DLP/SIEM connections, zero data retention, CSM, domain management

pricing.notes: Custom Agents free to try, then $10 per 1,000 monthly Notion credits. Notion Workers free to try, start using credits on August 11. Yearly billing offers up to 20% off. Some plans (Plus, Enterprise) marked as 'may not be available for every workspace'.

Billing / pricing
Billing FAQ
Special plans for

or

Enterprise plans
Contact Sales
Frequently asked questions
How does Airtable's pricing work?
Airtable plans are charged per seat. If you are on Airtable’s Free plan, all users are free. If you are on a Team or Business plan, you will be charged for all users who have edit permissions for at least one base in the workspace. No charges will apply on a Team or Business plan for read-only collaborators, form submissions, or share links. Charges are prorated: users with edit permissions added partway through the month will only be charged for the days after they were added.
How does Airtable's Business and Enterprise Scale pricing work?
Airtable has two plans geared towards large company usage of Airtable: Business and Enterprise Scale. You can purchase Business either through the plan upgrades page in your workspace settings tab, or by contacting our sales team. To purchase Enterprise Scale, please contact sales. On either plan, you will be charged for all users with edit permissions for at least one base in your organization. As part of the Enterprise Scale plan, an organization has the ability to create unlimited org units, workspaces and bases within their organization. Charges are prorated: collaborators with edit permissions added partway through the billing cycle will only be charged for the months after they were added. For more information on our Business and Enterprise Scale plans,
contact sales
.
Is Airtable free to use?
Airtable has multiple packages to help teams of all sizes orchestrate their work. Our Free plan is available to teams for no charge and provides the key building blocks to help you build applications. The Free plan is formulated for individual users, very small teams, or those with lightweight needs. Our paid plans offer additional power and scale for teams, departments, and large enterprises.
How much does Airtable cost?
Our Free plan is available at no cost for users just getting started. For teams who like the features of our Free plan but need more capacity, our Team plan is available for $20/user/month when bi

analysis.name: Airtable
analysis.tagline: All your teams, all their workflows—connected in one workspace
analysis.value_proposition: A no-code app-building platform that unifies data, AI, and workflows so business teams can build enterprise-grade applications without engineering.
analysis.ideal_customer: Cross-functional business teams at mid-market and enterprise orgs (product, marketing, ops, sales, HR, finance) needing custom apps and AI workflows on top of relational data without code.

praised: Strong community interest in the spreadsheet-database hybrid model (HN: 'Show HN: Airtable, a real-time spreadsheet-database hybrid' — 361 upvotes)
Category-defining enough to spawn multiple open-source alternatives (Baserow, NocoDB, Grist) — validation of the core UX
complaints: Demand for self-hosted alternatives implies lock-in / hosting concerns (HN: 'Baserow.io – Self-hosted Airtable alternative' — 802 upvotes)
Open-source alternatives cited as cheaper/more flexible (HN: 'NocoDB – Open-Source Airtable Alternative' — 562 upvotes)
Microsoft entering the space with Lists suggests perceived pricing/positioning pressure (HN: 'Microsoft announces Lists, a new Airtable-like app' — 531 upvotes)

free_tier.limits: Up to 5 editors, unlimited bases, 1,000 records/base, 1 GB attachments/base, 100 automation runs, 500 AI credits/editor/mo

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited bases, 1k records/base, up to 5 editors, Interface Designer, 500 AI credits/editor

tiers.name: Team
tiers.headline_inclusions: 50k records/base, 25k automations, extensions, Gantt/timeline, standard sync, 15k AI credits

tiers.name: Business
tiers.headline_inclusions: 125k records/base, premium sync, two-way sync, SAML SSO, App Sandbox, admin panel, 20k AI credits

tiers.name: Enterprise Scale
tiers.headline_inclusions: 500k records/base, HyperDB, Enterprise Hub, App Library, EKM/DLP/audit, Enterprise API, 25k AI credits

pricing.notes: Team monthly $24, Business monthly $54 (annual pricing shown as $20/$45); Portals sold as add-on starting from $120 (Team) / $150 (Business) for 15 guests/month; AI credits allocated per paid user per month; monthly billing available for Team/Business tiers.

Growth & Acquisition Update for the Week of April 21
This week, we are excited to report that our Paid Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) has shown remarkable strength, now standing at an impressive 4.2x in search. This marks a significant increase from last week's figure of 3.8x, indicating that our advertising strategies are effectively resonating with our target audience. Additionally, our activation rate has reached 38%, which is a notable rise of 4 points compared to last month. This is particularly encouraging as it represents the highest activation rate we have seen since December, showcasing our ongoing efforts to enhance user engagement and conversion.

Privacy Preference Center
When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences, or your device, and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect. The information does not usually identify you directly, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to learn more and change our default settings. Blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.

Performance Cookies
Always Active

If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.

Cookie Preferences
Privacy Preference Center
When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences, or your device, and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect. The information does not usually identify you directly, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to learn more and change our default settings. Blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.
More information
Allow All
Manage Consent Preferences
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.
Performance Cookies
Always Active
These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. All information these cookies collect is aggregated and therefore anonymous. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.
Functional Cookies
Always Active
These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.
Targeting Cookies
Always Active
These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They do not store directly personal information, but are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.

integrations.title: ClickUp™ | Integrations
integrations.text:
ClickUp™ | Integrations
Brain AI
Product
Solutions
Learn
Pricing
Enterprise
Get a Demo
Login
Sign Up
Connect over 1,000+ tools to ClickUp for free
Welcome to the future of productivity, where useful features and integrations are shipped to you weekly.
Start integrating
<
Enter terms to filter:
Filter
Native integrations
Automatic Import
More Integrations
Native integrations
Built by ClickUp, loved by everyone.
ClickUp API
Build custom ClickUp integrations and apps using the public API for tailored automation and data synchronization.
Slack
Create and update ClickUp Tasks from Slack messages, unfurl links with rich details and actions, and receive real-time task updates in Slack channels.
GitHub
Sync commits, branches, pull requests, and issues between GitHub and ClickUp to track development progress without switching tools.
Zapier
Automate workflows between ClickUp and thousands of apps by setting triggers and actions, no coding required.
GitLab
Link GitLab commits, branches, pull requests, and issues with ClickUp Tasks for real-time visibility and streamlined development.
Webhooks
Send real-time ClickUp data to external apps and services by triggering automated workflows through Webhooks.
HubSpot
Sync CRM data, tickets, and properties between ClickUp and HubSpot to track pipeline progress and manage projects seamlessly.
Everhour
Track time on ClickUp Tasks, generate reports, and manage budgets with Everhour’s integrated time-tracking.
Toggl
Log time directly from ClickUp Tasks using Toggl, ensuring accurate tracking and reporting across projects.
Harvest
Connect ClickUp with Harvest to track time spent on Tasks and sync logged hours for invoicing and reporting.
Google Drive
Attach, search, and preview Google Drive files directly in ClickUp Tasks, Docs, and Chat for seamless file access.
OneDrive
Attach and manage OneDrive files within ClickUp Tasks, Docs, and Chat to keep project assets organized.
Dropbox
Embed and preview Dropbox files in ClickUp Tasks, Docs, and Chat, keeping work files easily accessible.
Outlook
Convert Outlook emails into ClickUp Tasks or create new Tasks without leaving your inbox.
Google Calendar
Sync ClickUp Tasks with Google Calendar to manage schedules, track deadlines, and update events in real time.
Sync with Calendars
Enable one-way sync to automatically update Apple and Outlook calendars with ClickUp Tasks.
SSO
Log in to ClickUp securely using Google, Microsoft, or Okta accounts for simplified access management.
Figma
Embed interactive Figma prototypes in ClickUp Tasks, Docs, and Chat for real-time design collaboration.
Vimeo
Embed Vimeo videos in ClickUp Tasks, Docs, and Chat to review and share video content seamlessly.
YouTube
Paste YouTube links in ClickUp Tasks, Docs, and Chat to embed and watch videos directly in your Workspace.
Loom
Embed Loom screen recordings in ClickUp Tasks, Docs, and Chat to share updates and feedback instantly.
Zoom
Start and join Zoom meetings from ClickUp Tasks, Docs, and Chat with automatic meeting details and recordings.
Bitbucket
Sync Bitbucket commits, branches, and pull requests with ClickUp Tasks for better visibility into development work.
Chrome
Create ClickUp Tasks, capture screenshots, and save emails from Gmail and Outlook using ClickUp’s Chrome extension.
Sentry
Convert Sentry error reports into ClickUp Tasks, ensuring developers can track and resolve bugs efficiently.
Microsoft Teams
Sync ClickUp with Microsoft Teams to create tasks, receive updates, automate meeting notes, and collaborate in real time.
Google Hangouts Chat
Get real-time ClickUp Task notifications directly in Google Chat to keep project updates visible.
Intercom
Create and link ClickUp tasks directly from Intercom conversations to streamline customer support workflows.
Box
Attach Box files to ClickUp tasks and Docs to centralize document management and collaboration.
Zendesk
Link, create, and track ClickUp Tasks from Zendesk tickets to align customer support with project execution.
Front
Connect ClickUp with Front to create, link, and manage support tickets and tasks in one Workspace.
Clockify
Track time on ClickUp tasks using Clockify and sync data for better time management and reporting.
Timely
Automatically track time on ClickUp tasks with Timely's AI engine, linking logged hours to tasks for accurate reporting.
TMetric
Use the TMetric Chrome extension to track time spent on ClickUp tasks and optimize workload distribution.
Timeneye
Track time on ClickUp Tasks using Timeneye and sync data for accurate billing and reporting.
PomoDone
Use the Pomodoro technique to track time on ClickUp Tasks and stay focused with structured work intervals.
TimeCamp
Sync TimeCamp with ClickUp to track time, generate invoices, and analyze reports for better project insights.
Time Doctor
Monitor productivity by tracking time on ClickUp tasks with Time Doctor’s desktop and browser app.
LambdaTest
Perform automated and live browser testing with LambdaTest and log bug reports as ClickUp tasks.
DueFocus
Report tracked time directly from DueFocus into ClickUp tasks for accurate project insights.
Twilio
Automate SMS notifications from ClickUp tasks using Twilio, dynamically inserting custom field data.
Bugsnag
Sync Bugsnag error alerts with ClickUp tasks to track and resolve application bugs efficiently.
Calendly
Automatically create ClickUp Tasks from new Calendly events, capturing meeting details in one place.
Miro
Embed Miro whiteboards in ClickUp tasks, Docs, and Chat for real-time brainstorming and planning.
Discord
Send ClickUp notifications to Discord channels to keep teams updated on project changes.
Tableau Web Connector
Sync ClickUp data with Tableau to create dynamic dashboards for real-time reporting.
Giphy
Insert Giphy images in ClickUp Docs and comments to enhance team communication.
Automatic import from other tools
Migrate to ClickUp in minutes. Bring everything over with just a few clicks!
Asana Import
Import tasks, projects, and attachments from Asana into ClickUp in minutes—no data left behind. Keep your entire workflow in one place and customize exactly what you bring over.
Trello Import
Bring your Trello boards, cards, and due dates into ClickUp with just a few clicks. Enjoy advanced features while keeping all your tasks intact.
Todoist Import
Import tasks from Todoist—complete with due dates and priority levels. Manage them all in ClickUp, alongside powerful automation and collaboration.
Jira Import
Migrate your Jira issues, sprints, and epics into ClickUp seamlessly. Keep dev teams aligned with no data lost in the transition.
Monday.com Import
Move your Monday.com boards to ClickUp in one go—tasks, owners, and statuses included. Get more done with more powerful tools, flexible features, and an AI-powered Workspace that adapts to your needs.
Basecamp Import
Import your Basecamp to-dos, discussions, and files into ClickUp. Keep context and tasks together for streamlined collaboration.
Wrike Import
Transfer tasks, folders, and user assignments from Wrike into ClickUp in a snap. Centralize your workflow and unlock more advanced tools, flexible features, and AI-powered insights and automation.
Confluence Import
Import Confluence pages into ClickUp Docs for centralized knowledge management and AI-powered Connected Search.
Third-party integrations
Connect ClickUp to 1,000+ tools with integrations built by other companies.
OpenAI
OpenAI's ChatGPT can tap into your ClickUp workspace to give you AI-powered insights about your tasks, projects, and conversations.
Grammarly
Get grammar suggestions and spelling checks in any ClickUp text field.
Unito
Sync ClickUp with Jira, Trello, HubSpot, and more—enabling real-time updates across teams and departments.
Canny
Connect Canny posts to ClickUp tasks to inform your roadmap and automatically update customers on feature status.
Shift
Use Shift to navigate between your ClickUp tasks, email, calendars, and drive accounts—all in one desktop client.
CloudApp
Create visual explanations and insert them into ClickUp task descriptions, comments, and more in seconds.
Make
Connect ClickUp with hundreds of apps to automate your workflows—no coding required.
SupportBee
Manage product support requests and help tickets in SupportBee, then sync them seamlessly to ClickUp.
EasyInsight
Generate grids, pivot tables, charts, and tree reports with your ClickUp data—drilling down from Spaces to the tasks that matter most.
Station
Unify all your work apps in a single interface, eliminating countless browser tabs and letting you switch between them quickly.
Protractor plugin
Use the clickup-protractor-plugin to integrate test reporting into your team’s workflow by linking tests directly to ClickUp tasks.
Asana
Sync tasks from Asana to ClickUp for more powerful project management across your teams.
Basecamp
Bring tasks from Basecamp into ClickUp, unifying your project management in one place.
Trello
Automatically send new Trello cards to ClickUp so your tasks stay in one place.
Jira
Create new tasks in ClickUp whenever an issue is created in Jira with a specific tag—keeping teams aligned.
Monday
Sync pulses from Monday to ClickUp, creating tasks automatically and streamlining your project management.
Google Sheets
Export ClickUp data to Google Sheets automatically for quick analysis and reporting.
Ring Central
Automatically create tasks in ClickUp for each missed RingCentral call, ensuring timely follow-ups.
Evernote
Convert Evernote notes into ClickUp tasks, so you can organize and track them more effectively.
Google Forms
Turn new Google Form submissions into ClickUp tasks, streamlining your intake process.
Helpscout
Automatically create tasks in ClickUp for new Help Scout conversations, bridging customer support and task management.
Typeform
Convert new Typeform entries into ClickUp tasks to capture details accurately and follow up promptly.
Intercom
Create a ClickUp task whenever a lead becomes a new user in Intercom, ensuring client details are recorded and addressed.
Salesforce
Automatically create ClickUp tasks for new Salesforce opportunities, helping you follow up faster and close more deals.
Zoho Flow
Integrate ClickUp with 300+ apps—no code required. Automate repetitive tasks so you can focus on what matters.
Userback
Send visual website feedback directly into ClickUp tasks, eliminating manual copying and pasting.
Slab
Embed ClickUp tasks in Slab to view titles, statuses, and descriptions—track tags and due dates without leaving Slab.
WebWork Time Tracker
Track time on ClickUp tasks with WebWork to keep projects on schedule and boost productivity.
Airtable
Bring your Airtable bases into ClickUp to collaborate on data alongside your tasks.
Hubstaff
Track time on ClickUp tasks with Hubstaff, automate business processes, and improve productivity.
Memtime
Memtime automatically tracks your time in all programs and displays your workday, helping you accurately log every minute.
Pabbly Connect
Automate your ClickUp workflows with Pabbly Connect. Create triggers, add filters, and map fields—no coding needed.
Marker.io
Capture your screen, add visual annotations, and report issues in ClickUp—all directly from your website.
Integrately
Connect ClickUp with 300+ apps to automate cross-app communication and simple workflows in a breeze.
n8n
Use n8n’s open, fair-code workflow automation to connect ClickUp with any app you need.
Monitask
A 5-in-1 remote time-tracking toolkit that helps teams monitor productivity and stay efficient in ClickUp.
TestLodge
Automatically create ClickUp tasks for failed tests with TestLodge, giving developers all the details they need to fix issues.
Sunsama
Plan your day with Sunsama and ClickUp. Browse tasks by Space or Folder, drag them into your schedule, and automate your workflow.
ProBackup
Secure your ClickUp data with daily, automated backups of Spaces, tasks, and files.
SaveMyLeads
Automatically create tasks, Lists, or Folders in ClickUp from new Facebook leads—no manual data entry required.
PractiTest
Manage your QA efforts in real-time with PractiTest. Sync and report tasks between PractiTest and ClickUp for seamless test coverage.
Merge.dev
Use Merge.dev’s unified API to read and write data across ClickUp and other project management tools on behalf of your users.
Sleekplan
Sync user feedback and status updates with ClickUp by creating tasks directly from your Sleekplan dashboard.
Jotform
Collect project info, accept payments, and automate workflows in ClickUp with Jotform.
Morgen
Time block ClickUp tasks on any calendar, protect time for your priorities, and sync tracking back to ClickUp.
Aikido
Consolidate nine security scanners into one platform with Aikido Security. Track and resolve vulnerabilities directly in ClickUp.
Relay
Automate your ClickUp tasks with Relay.app, a flexible platform offering deep integrations and advanced workflow features.
Timing
Automatically import your ClickUp tasks and track your time spent. Timing syncs logs back to ClickUp and shows when tasks are completed.
Rize
Rize is an automatic time tracker that helps you track clients and projects so you can stay in flow and move faster.
Unthread
Sync ClickUp tasks with Unthread's AI-native helpdesk that tracks and automatically resolves issues from Slack & Teams.
Screenful
Instant Analytics and Automated Reports for ClickUp, Linear, GitHub, and more.
Custom Integrations
Embed web apps or build your own integration with ClickUp's API
Connect any tool with ClickUp’s Open API or simply embed a web app inside ClickUp with Embed View.
ClickUp API
Customizable integrations
Connect any tool with ClickUp’s Open API. Embed, sync, and automate workflows with custom integrations that fit your needs.
Learn more
Embed View
Embed web apps
Integrate over 50 external apps into your Workspace with Embed View—embed popular tools like Google Maps, YouTube, and Salesforce dashboards right alongside your tasks, so you can save time and minimize context switching.

tiers.name: Unlimited
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited storage, Gantt, integrations, custom fields, time tracking, goals, chat

tiers.headline_inclusions: Full agentic suite: Notetaker, AI Fields, Automations, Image Gen, 5,000 credits/mo

tiers.name: AI Super Credits
tiers.headline_inclusions: $10 per 10,000 credits ($0.001/credit) — fuels agents, AI fields, automations, image gen

tiers.name: ClickUp Assist
tiers.headline_inclusions: 2 hours 1:1 expert AI setup time per month

pricing.notes: Seat-based. Up to 30% yearly discount on core plans, 20% yearly on AI add-ons. Automations metered per month (5K Business, 250K Enterprise). AI usage metered in Super Credits with Super Fair Billing (rate adjustments passed through). Whole workspace must upgrade — cannot upgrade individual seats. 100% money-back guarantee. Image generation subject to fair use policy.

Your Privacy Choices
We use cookies to give you the best experience when using our website. To learn more about our cookie use, see our
Cookie Notice
Your Privacy Choices
Reject All
Accept Cookies
Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Data
When you visit our website, we store cookies on your browser to collect information. The information collected might relate to you, your preferences or your device, and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to and to provide a more personalized web experience. However, you can choose not to allow certain types of cookies, which may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings according to your preference. You cannot opt-out of our First Party Strictly Necessary Cookies as they are deployed in order to ensure the proper functioning of our website (such as prompting the cookie banner and remembering your settings, to log into your account, to redirect you when you log out, etc.). For more information about the First and Third Party Cookies used please follow this link.
Cookie Notice
Allow All
Manage Consent Preferences
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.
Analytics and Customization Cookies
Analytics and Customization Cookies
Allow cookies on the Coda website that help us analyze traffic and improve your experience.
Share or Sale of Personal Data
Share or Sale of Personal Data
As a California consumer, you have the right to opt-out from the sale or sharing of your personal information at any time across business platform, services, businesses and devices. You can opt-out of the sale and sharing of your personal information by using this toggle switch. As a Virginia, Utah, Colorado and Connecticut consumer, you have the right to opt-out from the sale of your personal data and the processing of your personal data for targeted advertising. You can opt-out of the sale of your personal data and targeted advertising by using this toggle switch. For more information on your rights as a United States consumer see our privacy notice.
Advertising (Targeting) Cookies
Switch Label

FAQs
What is a "Workspace"?
A workspace is the home for you and your team in Coda. It will store your docs in an organized way, and you can invite Doc Makers and Editors into your workspace to collaborate all in one place. For administrators, this is the place where you'll configure your team's settings and manage your account.
What is a "Doc Maker"?
Doc Makers can create and manage docs, pages, folders, and workspaces.
Specifically — in workspaces, Doc Makers have the power to create new docs and templates and create and manage folders to organize the workspace. Within docs, Doc Makers can administer things like adding pages, changing doc and page titles and icons, adjusting the timezone, configuring doc and page locking etc. Coda AI is also included for Doc Makers.
Editors can edit content and collaborate in existing docs & pages, including adding tables & views, constructing formulas & buttons, adding and configuring Packs & automations, etc.
Learn more
Who do I pay for and why?
Upgrading a free workspace to a paid plan can be done with a single Doc Maker or can include your whole team to start. As you grow into Coda, you can invite additional members to become Editors or Doc Makers in your workspace. Editors and Viewers are always free.
At the end of each billing cycle, you will be billed for the workspace based on how many Doc Makers are in the workspace. Any changes to the number of Doc Makers in the workspace mid-cycle are automatically prorated.
How much does Coda AI cost?
Coda AI is included for Doc Makers, and Editors also receive a free trial. Based on your plan and number of Doc Makers, each workspace receives a set of credits each month, that are pooled across all Doc Makers.
Learn more
What is a credit?
Credits are Coda's way of counting how much AI you use. You could think about credits as fragments of text. 1 credit is approximately 40 characters in English, or 7.5 words. Anytime you use Coda AI, we measure how much content is in the input + the output and convert those into credits. So if you have a huge block of text and ask for a small summary, your number of credits will be based on the large input + small output. If you have a small prompt asking AI to write a long essay, you'll have a small input + large output. And if you have a large input and large output, you'll add those lengths together to get your total credits used for that request.
How can I get more AI credits?
If you have used all of your allotted credits, or would like to access more, you can add more Doc Makers to your workspace, upgrade your plan, or purchase an AI credit add-on. You can purchase 2,000 credits for $2/Doc Maker/Month, 6,000 credits for $6/Doc Maker/month, or unlimited credits for $12/Doc Maker/month (billed monthly).
Learn more
Can I give edit access to people outside of my workspace?
Yes! Whenever you share a doc with someone outside of your workspace, they will be added as a Guest Editor (though you can turn this ability off if you'd like). Guest Editors can edit content in individual docs shared with them, but cannot add and manage pages, or browse the doc's workspace or folder. Guest Editors are free, just like Editors in your workspace.
All Editors and Viewers in a workspace are free! Viewers can open any shared link to a doc and view and comment on it. Just like Guest Editors, Viewers are also guests and cannot see the full workspace or folder.
What happens when I publicly share my doc?
You can always share a doc to anyone with a link through the sharing feature inside the doc.
Learn more
When will I get billed for my workspace?
When you sign up for a new plan, as well as when it renews, you will be billed up front for all the users in your workspace.
Whenever you add a new paid user to your workspace (or upgrade an existing one), we'll prorate them for the remainder of your billing cycle. On the annual plan, you will be charged monthly for these additional membership changes, with prorations applied for the remainder of the billing year. Additionally, you will be charged whenever your bill exceeds $1,000 from adding new members.
Do I get a refund if I remove members from my workspace or cancel my plan?
When you remove members or cancel your plan, we'll apply a prorated credit onto your account you can use for future charges in your workspace.
Do you offer discounts for students, educators, non-profits, or startups?
Yes!
Students and educators: We offer several discounts, depending on your needs.
If you're a student, get started
here
.
If you're an educator, get started
here
.
Non-profits: Qualifying organizations can receive a 50% discount. Get started
here
.
Startups: Eligible startups can get the Team plan free for 6 months for their workspace. Apply
here
.
How does Enterprise pricing work?
You only pay for Doc Makers, even on Enterprise. With increased scalability, security and support, Enterprise plans are a great pick if you're planning to roll out Coda across multiple teams or departments. Coda's pricing scales to fit enterprise teams at every stage and we work with you to find the custom fit for your situation.

Share this article
Stay in the loop with Coda's rapid evolution!
At Coda, we believe in constant innovation and continuous improvement. And we're excited to share our latest feature launches.
May 2026
AI Column Improvements
AI Columns are now powered by the latest, most capable models for a meaningfully better experience. The outputs are faster, more accurate, and more consistent. Just describe what you want a column to do, and Coda handles the rest.
April 2026
Coda MCP Public Beta
Connect your workspace to AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, and Cursor with a single OAuth connection, and start reading, writing, and updating your docs and tables directly from the AI tools you already use.
Get started
Visualize progress on timeline view items
Select a column to represent progress on your timeline view items, and Coda will shade them accordingly as work moves forward.
Point & compose in tables
Building formulas just got faster. Click any column header or cell to insert it into your formula automatically, or hold CMD to reference a specific cell value. No more typing column names from memory.
New tools for managing doc size
A refreshed doc statistics panel now shows total size with more detail on what's contributing to it, the doc map surfaces automation and column-level references, and new in-product guidance covers the most common size culprits and how to address them.
February 2026
Coda for Intune v1.0.0
Coda for Intune is now available on the App Store. Built with full Intune SDK integration, it supports MAM/MDM policies including domain locking, authentication provider configuration, and screenshot prevention.
New design for importers
We’ve refreshed the import flow from Notion, Asana, Confluence, and Quip into Coda with updated visual polish and improved usability. This includes refined support links, a cleaner and better-sized import dialog UI, and clearer visibility of recently imported pages within the workspace.
January 2026
Tabbed views
Now you can bring your table, calendar, board, and timeline together in one place. No more jumping between pages or scrolling through your doc. Switch between views with just a click.
December 2025
Markdown paste options
Pasted markdown content now displays as formatted text by default, with options to switch to plain text or code blocks. This works across canvas, table cells, callouts, and comments.
November 2025
Page list accessibility improvements
We've improved accessibility across Coda’s navigation with skip links, better keyboard support, enhanced contrast and focus states, and tooltips throughout. These updates make it easier for everyone to move through their docs with confidence.
Icon picker accessibility improvements
We refreshed the icon experience with clearer active states, consistent tooltips, improved section hierarchy, refined tabs, and visual polish—including a gradient scroll hint and a cleaner, more spacious search bar.
October 2025
Folder UI updates
Now, a consistent folder icon makes for a visually cleaner folder list and easier differentiation between docs.
Move and copy dialogue
We’ve improved our move and copy dialogues so you no longer have to ‘star’ a folder before it appears as an option. Search all of your folders across workspaces for a seamless organization experience.
Learn more
Box Pack
Sync files, manage folder permissions, and access file metadata between Box and Coda to streamline content collaboration and file governance workflows.
Check out the Pack
Google Meet Pack
Connect Google Meet meetings and content directly into your Coda docs to streamline scheduling, planning, and follow-up.
Check out the Pack
September 2025
UI Refresh
We’ve refreshed the UI to make docs easier to navigate and work in. Menus and settings are now consolidated, advanced tools are better organized, and navigation flows more smoothly, giving new users a simpler start and experienced makers a sharper workspace.
Get the details
August 2025
Media improvements
Play .mp4 and .mov files directly from your page. We also gave our audio embeds a refreshed look.
Triangle of safety
Move between table menus without them closing.
Additional canvas improvements
URL updates
Now, the URL changes each time you select a component on the canvas. This allows you to share specific parts of a page more easily with your team.
Links in canvas cells
Edit links directly in a canvas cell when using text preview.
New first party Outlook Packs
The Outlook
Mail
and
Calendar
Packs allows you to sync emails, folders, and calendars; create and send messages; and manage calendar events between Outlook and Coda, streamlining communication, scheduling, and planning, right from your doc.
New for Enterprise
Advanced sharing controls
Advanced external sharing controls allow you to configure domain-level sharing allow/block lists and share individual docs without relaxing global policies. You can also set which sharing integrations your teams use for a cleaner sharing dialog.
Dive into the new controls
Improved folder permissions and sharing
Set precise access with granular roles: choose
Can edit
for content changes or
Can manage
for full sharing controls. Plus, share folders across workspaces or with trusted partners outside your org, always aligned with your sharing policy.
Explore roles and sharing
Manage folder access and visibility
The new central dashboard for admins simplifies managing folder access and monitoring visibility, reducing external risks.
Discover the dashboard
July 2025
More international number format options
Now you can format numbers based on your regional conventions, using different combinations of 1000s and decimal separators.
Learn more
New Common Room Pack
Reduce tool switching, operationalize engagement data, and speed up follow-up by integrating Common Room directly into Coda workflows. Import Common Room segments, contacts, organizations, and activity types into your docs.
Check out the Pack
Google Calendar Pack updates
With the new
FreeTimes
formula you can display a user's free time slots.
Check out the Pack
SetPageName( ) formula
You can now set your pages name via an action formula.
Learn more
SetPageVisibility( ) formula
You can now hide or show pages via an action formula.
Learn more
Hide or show pages support when using DuplicatePage( ) formula
When duplicating pages, you can set the visibility you like. For example, when duplicating a hidden template, you can make it visible.
Learn more
New for Enterprise
Two new admin roles for Enterprise
Enterprise organizations now have access to two additional admin roles:
Content Admins – Manage docs and folders within a designated workspace.
Org Pack Admins – Oversee Pack permissions and approvals across the entire organization.
Learn more
Workspace members export
Workspace admins can now export a full list of members to a .csv file directly from the Admin Panel, making it easier and faster to audit access and user roles.
Learn more
June 2025
Table Locking now in GA
Table Locking allows you to maintain data integrity by locking a table and all its views, while providing flexible options for collaborators to add, edit, or delete information.
Learn more
Automation references now visible in Doc map
It’s now easier to understand how automations use objects in your doc, like buttons, tables, and more, at a glance!
More Updates
More efficient personal controls
Personal control updates from a single user now only affect that user, not all users. All other users can continue working smoothly, even in large docs.
Faster API responses
The Coda APIs respond ~10x (in some cases 100x) faster to requests with reduced latency.
Optimized calculation invalidation
In rare cases, small changes can trigger a huge number of calculations. By analyzing the formula in a doc, we shortcut most calculations, reducing calculation time by multiple orders of magnitude and cutting memory usage by a third in these cases.
May 2025
Auto-embed .mp4 files
When you drag a .mp4 file onto the canvas, it will automatically embed into your doc.
Try it out in a new doc
Folders in Confluence importer
We've updated the Confluence Cloud importer to allow the import of folders that live inside spaces. Upon import, the hierarchy of those pages is now preserved.
Learn more about the importer
View number of pages before import
To help you manage performance and avoid overloading your documents, you can now see the number of pages being imported before the import begins.
Try the importer
Improved Notion zip import experience
We’ve made two key upgrades to make importing Notion content smoother:
Support for Large Files:
If your Notion export zip exceeds 2GB, it no longer fails right away. Instead, you can now upload the unzipped folder directly—bypassing browser limitations.
Selective Importing:
You can now choose which files to import from a Notion zip. Rather than importing the entire directory, simply select the pages you want and skip the rest.
Learn more about the importer
April 2025
Table Locking open beta
We’re excited to roll out Table Locking to improve your experience collaborating in tables. With Table Locking, you can lock a base table and its views, with options to prevent collaborators from adding, deleting, or editing table content.
Sign up
Playground Doc
We’re giving everyone a private place to test and tinker in Coda! Now, Editors and Makers alike will have access to their very own private Coda Playground doc.
Try it out
March 2025
Sync subitems in cross-doc tables
When setting up a new Cross-doc table, you can now choose to sync subitems if these are enabled in the original table, giving you more data visualization flexibility and consistency across your Coda docs.
Learn more
New Packs: Wrike and Azure DevOps
Reduce tool-switching and streamline even more data with our new Packs. Now you can integrate the Wrike Pack and/or Azure DevOps Pack directly into your Coda doc.
Wrike Pack
Reduce the back and forth by connecting your Wrike data with Coda. This Pack allows you to create and modify tasks directly in Coda, then see those updates reflected in Wrike—and vice versa.
Learn more
Azure DevOps Pack
Bridge your project management workflows in Coda with the Azure DevOps Pack. With two-way sync, this Pack enables you to access, manage, and edit tasks in one place.
Learn more
February 2025
Text visualization options
Two features offering more data visualization flexibility for text-heavy tables or card views.
Fixed row height for tables
You can now set a fixed row height for tables—choosing between one, two, three, or all lines—offering more flexibility for large tables.
Learn more
Line count options for card views
You can also choose between one, two, three, or all lines of text in a card view, and you can also select different options for different views.
Learn more
January 2025
Copy page links with personal controls
Create links that include your personal controls and table filter bar values so that when you send a link to your teammates, they see exactly what you do.
Learn more
Updates to groups
View your data in organized groups, while being able to make the updates you need—without messing it all up.
Learn more
Subitems
Make complex projects manageable by breaking them down into smaller sub-tasks.
Learn more
Improved in-doc search
Find what you're looking for faster. Searching across Coda docs, pages, and even workspaces is more accessible, accurate, and easy to navigate.
Learn more
Better linking between Coda docs
Linking between other Coda docs now displays the doc and page name. Linking between the same doc will show the page name and heading title.
Learn more
December 2024
Duplicate page hybrid option
When you duplicate a page with a mix of base tables and connected table views, the h

When you visit our website, we store cookies on your browser to collect information. The information collected might relate to you, your preferences or your device, and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to and to provide a more personalized web experience. However, you can choose not to allow certain types of cookies, which may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings according to your preference. You cannot opt-out of our First Party Strictly Necessary Cookies as they are deployed in order to ensure the proper functioning of our website (such as prompting the cookie banner and remembering your settings, to log into your account, to redirect you when you log out, etc.). For more information about the First and Third Party Cookies used please follow this link.

Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.

analysis.name: Coda
analysis.value_proposition: A collaborative doc-plus-database platform that combines writeups, hubs, trackers, and applications with AI and 600+ integrations to replace multiple point tools.
analysis.ideal_customer: cross-functional teams (product, sales, engineering, design, marketing, HR) at growing companies who want to consolidate docs, wikis, trackers, and lightweight apps

praised: "more powerful than Google Docs and more flexible than Airtable or Notion" (Fast Company)
Flexibility for building custom internal tools without engineering
complaints: Grammarly acquisition (2024) raised concerns about product direction on HN
Learning curve for the doc-plus-app hybrid model can be steep
Help center now redirects to Superhuman (Grammarly-owned) — potential confusion for existing customers

free_tier.limits: 1GB attachments per doc; low automation quota; 7-day version history; limited cross-doc; unlimited pages only for unshared docs

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: Collaborative docs, formulas, automations, free Packs, try AI

tiers.name: Pro
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited objects, 30-day history, hidden pages, custom domains, some AI credits

tiers.name: Team
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited automations & history, doc locking, cross-doc sync, group trainings, more AI

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: SAML SSO, SCIM, audit events, SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, legal hold, eDiscovery, most AI credits

pricing.notes: Maker Billing model: only Doc Makers pay; Editors and Viewers are always free. Prices are per Doc Maker per month. 15% off with annual billing. AI credits are tiered by plan. Pack pricing is stacked separately ($5/$10/$30 per Pack tier).

Since everything's in the same place and neatly organized in a relational database, AI can answer pretty challenging questions spanning multiple teams and processes:
Understanding > Productivity
Bring the data from the tools that you cherish into Fibery and connect with the rest of your workspace

Support
User guide and AI support
Chat and email
support
Our team of solution architects are happy to advise you on how to map your workflows into Fibery and answer any questions you have.
Migration

Chat with us

Billing

Available if you pay annually or the invoice amount exceeds $1000.
Multi-workspace
billing
Get a consolidated invoice and pay for each user once, regardless of how many workspaces they are in.
Custom contract
Questions & Answers
Is there a trial period?
Yes, every workspace gets a 14-day Pro plan trial. Fibery is powerful and takes time to explore. So you need time to procrastinate, receive some reminders, get into Fibery again, and, finally, "get" it. We think you can do it in 14 days. If you'd like to have a jump start, we suggest our
concierge support
.
What happens when the trial is over?
Once the trial is over, we'll ask you to make a decision: you can continue with Pro, select another paid plan, or switch to Free. No need to wait until the end of the trial period though: add billing details and select your plan at any point during the first 14 days to ensure uniterrupted experience.
Is there really no entities limit on the Free plan?
Yes, you can have any number of rows in your databases.  The performance depends on your usage patterns—for example, Tables handle large volumes of data better than Boards, and complex formulas take longer to calculate in large databases. If you have fewer than 50K entities, you'll almost certainly be fine. If you have more than 500K data points, consider aggregating them before adding to Fibery.
What about these 6 months for free for startups?
Get 6 months of Fibery for free if your company has fewer than 42 employees.
Ditch those scattered spreadsheets, and let’s grow together.
Learn more & apply
What happens if we invite/deactivate users when on a paid plan?
When you invite a new user, we charge you proportionally to the time left in the billing cycle. For example, if you pay annually and there are six months left until the next payment, the prorated amount is
$
12*(6/12)*12=
$
72.
When you deactivate a user, the unspent amount will be deducted from your next bill.
What payment methods do you accept?
We accept all major credit cards, PayPal, and Apple Pay. Pro and Enterprise customers can also pay via bank transfer. Credit cards and Paypal are available via self-service at checkout. Please contact us for Apple Pay or bank transfer payments.
How is the payment being processed?
We use Chargebee at the checkout and Braintree to process your payment.
Braintree
handles billions of dollars in transactions and is trusted by companies like Uber, Dropbox, and GitHub. We do not store or handle your credit card information directly.
Do you offer refunds?
We do. If you've made a mistake while signing up for a paid subscription, accidentally added paid members to your workspace, or simply lost enthusiasm for Fibery, please reach out to us to us via chat or at
new@fibery.io
within 30 days of payment.
Can we cancel our paid plan?
Sure, feel free to cancel your subscription at any time. All
