analysis.name: Fibery
analysis.tagline: Operating system for orgs run by nerds
analysis.value_proposition: A modular, no-code work platform that replaces scattered tools with one interconnected relational workspace for the entire company.
analysis.ideal_customer: Product, engineering, and cross-functional teams at startups, agencies, and mid-market orgs who want to build a custom connected workspace instead of stitching together SaaS tools.

features.name: relational databases
features.description: Core building block: custom databases with typed fields and cross-space relations.

features.name: table view
features.description: Spreadsheet-style view with hierarchy, inline edit, copy-paste, multiple data kinds.

features.name: board view
features.description: Kanban board with swimlanes and color coding.

features.name: list view
features.description: Hierarchical list view with inline edit.

features.name: calendar view
features.description: Calendar layout for date-based entities.

features.name: timeline view
features.description: Timeline with actual vs. planned dates and dependency-aware rescheduling.

features.name: gantt view
features.description: Gantt chart with dependencies and collapsible hierarchy.

features.name: gallery view
features.description: Card-based gallery layout.

features.name: feed view
features.description: Social-media-style scrollable feed for notes, briefs, or specs.
features.evidence_quote: Imagine a social media feed but for your notes, briefs, or specs.

features.name: map view
features.description: Satellite/map view of geo-tagged data.

features.name: form view
features.description: Forms for structured data intake with required and hidden pre-selected fields.
features.evidence_quote: Form — Required fields, Hidden pre-selected fields

features.name: dashboard view
features.description: Composable dashboards combining multiple views on one screen.

features.name: charts and reports
features.description: Built-in BI tool: bar charts, scatterplots, tables from live or historical data.
features.evidence_quote: Charts — We have a custom-made BI tool inside Fibery

features.name: historical data reporting
features.description: Charts include historical data on Standard and above.

features.name: docs
features.description: Collaborative rich-text documents inside the workspace.

features.name: whiteboards
features.description: Freeform whiteboards for brainstorming and diagramming.

features.name: smart folders
features.description: Dynamic folders that organize the sidebar around teams, objectives, or clients.
features.evidence_quote: Dynamic folders organizing the sidebar around your teams, objectives, or clients.

features.name: entity views
features.description: Customizable per-record layouts; rules to pick view based on entity state.
features.evidence_quote: customize how you see a database record when you open it. Some call it layouts.

features.name: view rules
features.description: Conditional entity view selection based on rules.

features.name: formulas
features.description: Spreadsheet-like formulas across databases and relations.

features.name: automations
features.description: No-code automations triggered by data changes.

features.name: js automations
features.description: JavaScript-based custom automations for advanced workflows.

features.name: unlimited automations
features.description: Removed automation caps on Enterprise plan.

features.name: integrations
features.description: Native connectors that pull external tool data into Fibery.

features.name: unlimited integrations
features.description: Removed integration caps on Enterprise plan.

features.name: custom connectors
features.description: Build custom integrations via developer APIs.

features.name: integration platforms
features.description: Support for iPaaS-style middle-layer platforms.

features.name: email integration
features.description: Email-in / email-to-entity ingestion.

features.name: fibery ai

features.description: SOC2-caliber security posture marketed on landing.

differentiators: Modular no-code platform that replaces many tools with one connected workspace
Relational database core with cross-space relations
AI included on every plan (including Free) plus MCP server
Free observers and free guests on all plans (only Admins/Members are billed)
Built-in BI/charts tool ('a product inside product')
Free tier for teams up to 10 users, not just individuals
Data residency choice across US, EU, UK, APAC

recent_ships.what: Changelog updated June 25, 2026 (specific entries not accessible; changelog page 404)
recent_ships.source: Header: 'Changelog: June 25, 2026'

praised: 'This seems a lot more powerful than Notion. I'm extremely surprised this isn't extremely popular.' — Reddit user quoted on landing
'Fibery allows me to break the silos between business processes… an organizational quantum leap!' — Mikael Klingvall, Dairdux
'Fibery costs almost as much as each of the four tools that we used previously.' — Nikita Fedoseev, enKod
Rated 4.8 on G2 and Capterra (per Fibery's own marketing)
Popular HN launch post: 705↑ / 145 comments
complaints: Implied learning curve — marketed as 'Powerful for those who can handle it' suggesting complexity
Reliability incident acknowledged: '2.5 hours downtime post-mortem' HN post
Self-described weakness: 'we are much better at product and engineering than at sales and marketing'

free_tier.limits: up to 10 users + 10 guests, 10 databases, 1 GB file storage, 14-day version history, private-only advanced views

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: 10 users, 10 databases, unlimited entities, docs, formulas, automations, integrations, AI

tiers.name: Standard
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited DBs, AI, Charts, Whiteboards, human support; free observers & guests

tiers.name: Pro
tiers.headline_inclusions: More AI, user groups, advanced permissions, JS automations, unlimited version history

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited automations & integrations, SAML SSO, SCIM, data residency, CSM

pricing.notes: Only Admins/Members are billed; Observers and Guests are free on every tier. Yearly billing saves 20%. Enterprise has a 25 paid-user minimum. 50% discount for non-profits, educational, and Ukrainian orgs; 100% discount for open-source. Startup program: 6 months free on Pro (up to $6,048 value).

Sales tracking
Close deals faster
Integrate Salesforce, Quickbooks, or HubSpot to manage leads, track opportunities, and keep your sales process proactive and results-driven.
Customer support management
Stay on top of every request
Connect Zendesk or Active Directory to manage your support tickets in monday.com, helping teams resolve issues faster and keep customers happy.
Code alignment
Keep development and delivery on track
Link pull requests from GitHub or GitLab directly to their related items in monday.com so teams can quickly access code and updates in context.
Team collaboration

Integrate your org’s communication tools - Slack, Gmail, Google Calendar, Outlook, Microsoft Teams, and more - to centralize communication and keep work flowing smoothly across channels.

analysis.name: Monday.com
analysis.value_proposition: AI work platform where people and agents drive results together on one secure, flexible workspace across PMO, sales, marketing, IT, HR, and dev.
analysis.ideal_customer: Cross-functional teams at SMB through Fortune 500 companies needing a customizable work platform spanning projects, CRM, dev, service, and marketing — with AI agents embedded in workflows.

features.evidence_quote: Boards are the place for organizing all your work and data (e.g. projects, clients, processes)

features.description: Collaborative real-time docs that can embed live board data via Real Time Engine.

praised: 'monday has all the functionalities and integrations we need without being unnecessarily complicated.' — Naomi M.
'monday.com's integrations with other software tools allow for seamless collaboration and project management across teams.' — Ray A.
'The integration available with Outlook has also helped ensure that important tasks received via emails are not missed' — Jim M.
HN interest in in-house data engine MondayDB signals engineering credibility
complaints: HN 'Ask HN: Does anyone use monday.com? Is it better than the others?' — signals uncertainty vs. alternatives
'Show HN: tillywork - opensource work management (monday.com alternative)' — signals users seeking OSS alternatives
'Ask HN: Did Monday.com's SendGrid service get compromised?' — deliverability/security concern in community

free_tier.limits: Up to 2 seats, 3 boards, 3 Docs, 200+ templates, 8 column types

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: Up to 2 seats, 3 boards, 3 Docs, 200+ templates, 8 column types, mobile apps

tiers.name: Basic
tiers.headline_inclusions: Basic AI (1,000 credits, 3 tools), unlimited free viewers, unlimited items

tiers.name: Standard
tiers.headline_inclusions: AI essentials (2,000 credits, 5 tools), 250 automations+integrations/mo, Timeline/Gantt/Calendar views, guest access, 1K API calls/day

tiers.name: Pro
tiers.headline_inclusions: Premium AI (3,000 credits, 6 tools), 25K automations+integrations/mo, private boards, advanced views/columns, time tracking, 10K API/day

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: Enterprise AI, custom AI credits, custom security/scale — contact sales

pricing.notes: Seat-based; prices shown at 10-seat sample. Yearly billing saves ~18%. Additional AI credits purchasable. API call limits per day scale by tier. Monthly special offer discounts ($10–$30 off/mo) shown on pricing page. Viewers are unlimited/free starting Basic. Automations/integrations have per-month action caps that scale by tier.

landing.title: Basecamp
landing.text:
Basecamp
We’d like to use cookies to help understand if our ads are working or not.

Yes, that’s fine

in Basecamp now

Pricing & sign up
— Two paid plans, one free plan
Basecamp 5 is here
—
Major upgrade for 2026
Features
— Remarkably simple, surprisingly capable
Paths
— Why people switch to Basecamp
API, CLI, Skills
— Developer tools, AI Agent-ready
Reliable to the core
— A multi-decade track record
160,635 people
are working in Basecamp right now!
The refreshingly straightforward project management system that’s rock-solid and easy to use.
Take a 3 minute tour of Basecamp
Simply put, we get more work done, quicker, and better.
Productivity is up. Errors are down. Clients are happier.
Patrick Sheffield, Moore Communications Group
Information flows like water. A lot more transparency. Everyone is on the same page.
No more secrets and blindspots.
Aaron Bingaman, Penn State Office of Emergency Management
Since using Basecamp, our
communication is drastically better and deadlines are met without drama.
Shannon Kropf, Full Sail University
I no longer have to wonder whether I’ve asked for something…
Every member of my team is more accountable.
John Drover, Roebothan McKay Marshall
We’re much better organised
and it’s way easier to deal with clients and projects.
Pedro Lopes, Coimbra Genomics
Basecamp makes us tighter as a group.
Basecamp makes it easy to create shared understanding in our company.
Teddy Zetterlund, Lägenhetsbyte
We don’t have to contact each other about every little thing.
When we need to know something, it’s right there.
Kelly Hunter, Chamber Nation
We used to wonder what everyone was doing.
Now we move forward gracefully.
John McCulley, Moxley Carmichael
It eliminated the need for a lot of extra communication and
reduced a lot of miscommunication between my team.
Anthony Clark, Clark Partners Realty Group
Action items never get lost in the shuffle anymore.
New ideas and projects actually get off the ground now.
Andrew Tyne, MPI Atlantic Canada Chapter
We are more organized. And because of this
we can turn around projects much more quickly now than before.
Kevin Duffy, TRUE Marketing
Check out over 1,000 more customer testimonials…
Tell me if this sounds about right.
Hey there—
There’s a good chance we know why you’re here.
You’re juggling people, projects, and expectations. There are tasks to do, discussions to have, decisions to make, files to share, deadlines to hit, relationships to manage, and work to deliver.
This all has to happen
somewhere
. But you’ve discovered that spreading everything across different apps, browser tabs, emails, and chats doesn’t work. It’s finally time to find a single system.
Unfortunately, most project management systems are bloated, complicated, and confusing. And software that’s hard to use doesn’t get used.
Over 20 years ago we were in the same boat. We needed something capable, but it had to be straightforward and easy. Nothing fit the bill. So we invented Basecamp, and we've been steadily improving it ever since. In software, longevity like this isn't luck — it's proof it works.
And BC5 is all new for 2026
.
Today, Basecamp is used across every industry, and nearly a million people, teams, companies, and non-profits worldwide
rely on it daily
.
Thanks for checking us out. We invite you to give Basecamp a try. And ultimately, we’d be honored to have you as a customer.
Jason Fried,
jason@basecamp.com
Co-founder & CEO
Big numbers. Highly-trusted. Rugged, reliable, and ready.
Over
84 million people
have Basecamp accounts.
Customers have managed more than
60 million projects
.
Over
1.7 billion to-dos
have been added.
Over
350 million message board posts
have been published.
Over
2.7 billion comments
have been posted.
More than
2.2 billion files
have been shared.
More than
5 petabytes
of data is stored by Basecamp.
And a
99.99% uptime record
that spans decades.
Remember when companies cared about service? We still do.
When’s the last time you praised a tech company’s customer service?
Our customers do it all the time
.
Service isn’t an afterthought, it’s one of Basecamp’s best features.
And get this: On average, each member of our customer service team has been with us 9 years. That’s dedication.
Meet Chase, our Head of Support
A stable, well-run business is part of the deal, too.
We’ve been running a business focused on the fundamentals for nearly three decades.
You can’t build reliable software unless you build a reliable company.
Founded in 1999, we’ve been profitable for 27 years in a row.
Zero debt, privately held, and built to stay, not exit.
99.99% historical
uptime
, with full transparency.
Cancel anytime, take your data with you, no lock in.
Direct access to our
CEO via email
.
All these questions have the same answer: Yes!
Can I
hide unfinished work from clients?
Can I
link up Google Docs, Figma, Dropbox, etc?
Can I
see all my tasks instantly anytime?
Can I
see everything that’s overdue on one screen?
Can I use Basecamp
if some teammates prefer email?
Can I
see all the work that was completed in a day?
Can I
set up projects that only some can see?
Can I
see a full history of someone’s work?
Can I
easily see someone else’s assignments?
Can I
see every deadline on a single calendar?
Can I
easily reference an entire conversation from last year?
Can I
follow a project without receiving notifications?
Can I
assign tasks to multiple people at once?
Can I
see all our projects laid out on a visual timeline?
Can I
follow-up with someone without anyone else knowing?
Can
our clients respond to our questions via email?
Can
our clients see a record of everything we’ve asked them?
Can
client feedback be documented for the record?
Can I
organize ideas and setup workflows on a Kanban board?
Can Basecamp
tell me when someone finished something?
Can Basecamp
help me get a client to approve a design?
Can Basecamp
keep my team accountable?
Check out the
full feature list
for more.
Join us for a free live class.
Curious about Basecamp but want to see it live and ask some questions first? Join us for a free live class.
We’ll show you how to set it up in minutes, how to roll it out to your team, and take Q&A covering anything else you’d like to ask.
See all upcoming classes
.

pricing.title: Basecamp — Pricing
pricing.text:
Basecamp — Pricing
We’d like to use cookies to help understand if our ads are working or not.
No, thanks
Yes, that’s fine
Pick a package and try Basecamp for free.
2,609
organizations signed up last week. You’re in good company.
Basecamp is beautiful software that has resisted every wrong trend and stayed true to the things that mattered most. Highly recommended.
Tobi Lutke
Shopify Co-Founder & CEO
Want a walkthrough? Live Q&A session?
Book a personal demo
with our team.
Start with a free account
Run 1 project free forever
. Includes 1 GB of storage and up to 20 users. If you ever need more you can upgrade later.
Sign up free
Or level up from the start with a Pro package
Pro Unlimited
Top-of-the-line, all-inclusive pricing. Unlimited users, no per-user fees.
$299/month, billed annually
Your whole organization for one fixed price.
Unlimited users, plus every feature & upgrade.
Try it free for 60 days
Unlimited projects
5 terabytes
storage space (10x Pro)
Fixed price — no per-user charges
Includes

?

?

with our team
?

24/7/365 customer support, your questions get answered first
?
Jump to the front of the queue when you contact support
More payment options
including credit card, check, ACH, or wire
Extended
60-day free trial
Pro
A great choice for freelancers, startups, and smaller teams.
$15/user, billed monthly
Invite guests and clients for free. We will only bill you for employees.
Try it free for 30 days
Unlimited projects
500 GB storage space
Pay-per-user
Optional upgrades sold separately
Self-guided onboarding
24/7/365 customer support
Pay by credit card
30-day free trial
The same core features are included with every package.
From Free to Pro Unlimited, you get
all the essential fundamentals built in.
Message Boards
for internal announcements
To-dos
for tracking work and accountability
Card Tables
is our take on Kanban for process tracking
Campfires & Pings
for group chat and direct messaging
Scheduling
for deadlines, milestones, and events
Docs & Files
for storing assets
Reports & Automatic Check-ins
for staying up on things
Rock-solid reliability
and a record you can trust
A stable, well-run company is part of the deal, too.
We’ve been running an upstanding, trustworthy, sustainable business for nearly three decades.
You can’t build reliable software unless you build a reliable company.
27 profitable, financially responsible years in business
Zero debt, privately held, and built to stay, not exit
99.99% historical uptime, with full transparency
Gold standard, famously great customer service
Extensive learning library free for all customers
Direct access to our CEO via email
Risk-free, cancel anytime, no long-term lock-in
With Basecamp, cancellation is entirely self-serve, no questions asked, no retention specialists trying to talk you out of it. Cancel any time, no long-term contracts to lock you in. Simple, straightforward, and fair, just as it should be.
A big boost for bootstrappers & small businesses
We started our business without outside funding, and you probably did too. We know your money’s precious, and we want to help. That’s why Basecamp is intentionally affordable for start-ups, freelancers, and small businesses just getting started.
Basecamp eliminates the Hassle Tax
There’s no more paying separate bills, onboarding people in multiple places, training people how to use different tools, managing expenses across vendors, etc… the benefits of Basecamp go beyond price.
I have pricing questions…
Can we upgrade, downgrade, switch packages, or cancel whenever we want?
Yes, you can upgrade
from Free to Plus or from Plus to Pro Unlimited whenever you’d like.
Yes, you can downgrade
from Pro Unlimited to Plus or Free as long as your usage fits on the lower package. For example, if you still had five projects, you couldn’t downgrade to the Free package which only allows one project. You’d have to delete four of those projects before downgrading.
Yes, you can cancel any time
, no questions asked, no forms to fill out, no person to talk to. If you cancel an annual package, the difference will be prorated back to your credit card.
Do you offer non-profit or educational discounts?
Absolutely.
Basecamp is 10% off for registered 501(c)(3) non-profits, and 100% free for K-12, homeschoolers, or Universities as long as it’s used for class work only.
For non-profits:
Email us
a copy of your 501(c)(3) paperwork right after you sign up. We’ll apply the discount once we verify your status.
For teachers and homeschool parents:
Right after you sign up,
email us
from your school account with the name of your school, the class you teach, and proof you’re a teacher. We’ll apply the discount after we verify everything.
For students:
Right after you sign up,
email us
from your school account with the name of your school, what you’re studying, and when you expect to graduate. We’ll apply the discount after we verify everything.
Note:
We can only discount one account. If you need to support multiple groups, one account will give you everything you need to support them. If you’ve been paying for Basecamp, we can’t refund or discount payments made before your discount started. We reserve the right to end the discount program due to chronic abuse of the free accounts or other circumstances. If we do, anyone who is already participating will continue to have their discounts applied. We just won’t offer up new free or discounted accounts.
Could we really add 1000 users and still just pay $299/month total on Pro Unlimited?
Yes.
Basecamp is one of the few products in the entire industry with an all-inclusive, unlimited users package for one fixed, capped price. We pioneered this all-in package, and have been offering it for over a decade.
If we have clients, or work with contractors, do we have to pay for them too?
No.
Clients and contractors can be added to your account for free. On the Plus package, we only bill you $15/user/month for employees or full users, not clients or temporary contractors.
Do I need a credit card to try Basecamp?
Nope.
We don’t require a credit card on the Free account, or for any of the free trials. If you choose a pay package with a free trial, you’ll be asked to enter your credit card at the end of your trial if you want to continue. It’s entirely up to you.
Will I automatically be charged when my 30 or 60-day free trial is up?
No.
We don’t ask for a credit card to try Basecamp, so we couldn’t charge you even if we wanted to. If you want to continue once your trial is up, then you’d enter your credit card. You’re in complete control.
How do upgrades work? What’s included with Timesheet or the Admin Pro Pack?
While Timesheet and Admin Pro Pack are included on the Pro Unlimited package, customers on the Plus package can optionally upgrade to either or both whenever they’d like.
Timesheet
allows you to track time spent on tasks, writing, meetings, or other pieces of work within a Basecamp project. You can then run reports to aggregate time spent across projects, or per-person, to help with resource allocation or client billing. You can also export the data to bring into an accounting or billing system of your choice. The Timesheet upgrade is $50/month flat no matter how many people you have on your account.
this video shows you how it works
.
Admin Pro Pack
gives account owners and admins greater control over access and permissions and allows them to:
Change who can send Pings (per company)
Change who can edit project details
Change who can edit people on a project
Change who can move, archive, and delete content
Change who can turn on public links
Limit editing and deleting comments & chat lines to 15 minutes after posting
Require two-factor authentication
Change chat history settings
Set Out of Office for others
The Admin Pro Pack upgrade is $50/month flat no matter how many people you have on your account. You can
learn more about Admin Pro Pack here
.
Can we buy more storage if we need it? How much is it?
Yes.
You can add an additional 1 terabyte (that’s 1000 gigabytes) of storage for $50/month flat. The storage can be used by all users across your entire account.
On the free one project package, can I delete the project and start another one?
Yes.
You can only run one project at time on the Free package, but if you complete a project, or just want to get rid of the one you’re currently running, you can delete it and free up the slot to start a new one. You can’t, however, keep more than one around. So in order to start a new one, you must delete the current one.
And there’s more…

integrations.title: Basecamp — Basecamp Integrations
integrations.text:
Basecamp — Basecamp Integrations
We’d like to use cookies to help understand if our ads are working or not.
No, thanks
Yes, that’s fine
Integrations for Basecamp.
Get more out of Basecamp with 3rd party tools! Are you a developer?
Integrate your product with Basecamp
.
Mobile and Desktop Apps
Assistant
Add Kanban Board View, Custom fields, Automations, Voice Messages and unread notification highlights to Basecamp. Organize work without leaving Basecamp.
Can Do!
Supercharge your Basecamp account. Publish blogs and knowledge bases, mass create, bulk archive, broadcast messages, Zoom, and much more!
CData Software
Use standards-based drivers to connect to Basecamp data in any BI tool, including Excel, Tableau, Power BI, and more!
elastic.io
Connect Basecamp with any app using elastic.io iPaaS. Automate tasks, sync projects, messages, and to-dos seamlessly across your workflows.
Field Trip
Share your team’s Basecamp activity in Slack and Google Hangouts Chat.
Monkedo
The Ultimate App for integration and automation. Seamlessly connect your favorite apps, automate tasks with ease, and create custom applications without coding.
Pleexy
Manage and update your Basecamp to-dos from your Todoist or Wunderlist.
Ply
Build Features into Basecamp based on your processes, data, and AI. Streamline team and customer workflows in a whole new way.
ReDD Do
Keep your to-dos from Basecamp in sight with floating task reminders, simple task lists, and easy time tracking. ReDD Do is open-source and collects zero user data.
Retool
The fast way to build internal tools. Pull in data from your own database and API, and build a tool to create new Basecamp to-dos from it.
RSSBus
Easily integrate Basecamp data with 50+ sources, including files, databases, or cloud applications.
SureTriggers
SureTriggers is a web-based automation tool that allows users to connect and automate processes between different online services, applications and WordPress plugins.
TaskClone
Automatically get Evernote & OneNote checklists and tasks in Basecamp.
Team O’clock
Run remote retrospectives with your teams, and create action items as Basecamp todos.
To-do Helpers
Create forms that submit directly to your Basecamp to-do lists or card tables. Auto-assign and auto-set due dates on your Basecamp to-dos.
Unito.io
Build your perfect workflow across tools and teams. Sync Basecamp to-do lists with Jira, Zendesk, HubSpot, Trello, Asana, Wrike, GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket.
Zapier
Automate your work day and connect Basecamp to 750+ business apps.
Zoho Flow
Stay productive by connecting Basecamp with 650+ apps like Jira Cloud, Slack, Zendesk and more.
Time Tracking, Invoicing, and Accounting
AssessTEAM
Continuous feedback based employee evaluation system & project profitability reporting suite.
Calamari
Modern HR and leave management system with iBeacon attendance tracking.
Centreli
The easiest way to manage employees, track vacation, sick, and paid time off.
clockcamp
Track Your Basecamp Card Table (Kanban) Time Using Clockify.
Clockify
The only truly free time tracker and timesheet app for teams. Add timers to your to-dos.
Everhour
Time tracking and estimating inside your to-dos. Highly flexible reporting opportunities.
Harvest
Track time without leaving Basecamp and let Harvest turn your time into insights.
Moon HRM
Time tracker, sprint and to-do synchronization for seamless collaboration.
My Hours

Bridge24

BrightGauge Software

DNS Check

Earned Value App

Beautiful dashboards and custom reports to keep your Basecamp projects on track.
Expiration Reminder

Recur

analysis.name: Basecamp
analysis.tagline: The refreshingly straightforward project management system that's rock-solid and easy to use.
analysis.value_proposition: A simple, all-in-one project management and team communication system with unlimited-user flat pricing and decades of reliability.
analysis.ideal_customer: Freelancers, startups, small-to-mid-size businesses, agencies, and non-profits who want one straightforward tool instead of a stack of apps.

praised: 'Productivity is up. Errors are down. Clients are happier.' — Patrick Sheffield, Moore Communications
'Information flows like water. A lot more transparency.' — Aaron Bingaman, Penn State
'Beautiful software that has resisted every wrong trend and stayed true to the things that mattered most.' — Tobi Lutke, Shopify
'Communication is drastically better and deadlines are met without drama.' — Shannon Kropf, Full Sail
Famously great customer service; simplicity vs. bloated PM tools
complaints: Public controversy over 2021 policy changes ('Changes at Basecamp', 883 HN comments)
~1/3 of employees accepted buyouts following the policy shift (1366 upvotes / 1965 HN comments)
HN interest in self-hosted alternatives suggests some users want more control/lower cost ('Show HN: self-hosted Basecamp alternative')
Perceived as opinionated/rigid — 'How Basecamp Blew Up' (372 upvotes / 903 comments)

free_tier.limits: 1 project, 1 GB storage, up to 20 users

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: 1 project, 1 GB storage, up to 20 users — forever free

tiers.name: Pro
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited projects, 500 GB, 24/7 support, free guests/clients, 30-day trial

tiers.name: Pro Unlimited
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited users/projects, 5 TB, Admin Pro Pack, Timesheet, priority support, 60-day trial

pricing.notes: Pro Unlimited billed annually only ($299/mo billed annually); Pro billed monthly at $15/user (employees only — guests/clients free). 10% off for 501(c)(3) non-profits; 100% free for K-12/homeschool/universities used for class work. Downgrades gated on usage fitting the lower tier's limits.

import React from 'react'
import { View, ActivityIndicator } from 'react-native'
import { useVehicleState, SyncStatus } from '@hooks/useVehicleState'
import { Dashboard } from '@components/Dashboard'

export const HomeScreen = () => {
  const { vehicleState, syncStatus } = useVehicleState()

  if (syncStatus === SyncStatus.PENDING) {
    return <ActivityIndicator size="large" />
  }

  return (
    <View>
      <Dashboard state={vehicleState} syncStatus={syncStatus} />
    </View>
  )
}

Changelog – Linear
Skip to content →

May 28, 2026
Linear Diffs
Elapsed
00:00
Seek to:
00:00
/

Agents generate large volumes of code, but individuals are still accountable for the changes that merge. This leads to a lot of review work, and the capability and performance of traditional review tools hasn’t kept up.
Today, we’re releasing Linear Diffs to make reviewing code a fast and fluid experience native to Linear.
You can now review diffs from any issue with a PR, iterate on further changes with agents, and ship code from Linear. All reviews in Linear sync back to GitHub, so the current state of review work is always clear.
Guided reviews (beta)
⁠
Guided reviews help you efficiently review large PRs. When you view a diff in this format, you’ll see the core of change first, so you don’t have to dig for an entrypoint.
Each section of diffs has an explanation — starting with what the change is, then moving into its consequences. Guided reviews separate out glue code and secondary changes so you can stay focused on what matters. This style of review makes it much easier to move through large changes in a single sitting.
Guided reviews are free during beta and available for Linear customers on Business and Enterprise plans.
Review Inbox
⁠
Staying on top of reviews is easier when review notifications live alongside the rest of your work. Linear notifies your Inbox for work like review requests, your approved PRs, and comment mentions. You can set up additional real-time desktop, Slack, email, or mobile
notifications
.
We’ve also added a dedicated Reviews tab in your sidebar so you can see all your review work at once. The default focus ordering sorts reviews by what’s closest to shipping, so the work that needs your attention most rises to the top.
Reviewing code
⁠
When a review calls for changes, keep iterating from the diff surface with a background coding agent. Agent changes update the diff in real-time, so you don’t need to check out the branch locally for every small tweak.
Diffs is responsive when reviewing even very large PRs. You can view unified or split diffs, enable line wrapping and structural highlighting, and choose custom code themes.
Linear Diffs is available now on all plans. Guided reviews are free during beta and available for Linear customers on Business and Enterprise plans.
Workspace admins can enable Diffs by
upgrading
Linear’s GitHub integration for code access. Then, individual users can choose to
review diffs in Linear
.
To learn more about why we built Diffs, read
Code review should be fast
. For more on Diffs functionality, check our
documentation
.
Fixes
Agents
Fixed choppy animation when expanding agent thoughts
Agents
Fixed agent chat incorrectly minimizing on external link click
Agents
Fixed the “Worked for X” label on agent session turns to measure active work time, excluding any idle wait between turns
Agents
Fixed agent panel staying focused when expanding or collapsing
Agents
Prevented the Ask Linear popup from reopening off-screen after the window height shrinks
Agents
Fixed Ask Linear drafts being lost when switching between chats
Agents
Fixed agent lists showing too many skills
Asks
Linear Asks Agent will now automatically reply to external users when requesting additional information
Asks
Fixed Asks Web submission attributions when the submitter logged into Asks Web before accepting their Linear workspace invite
Desktop
Fixed an issue where the desktop application could hang when waking up your computer from sleep
Desktop
Fixed desktop app flash of white background on new window open
Diffs
Fixed deduplication of linked PRs in issue attachment data
Diffs
Fixed refreshing the diff when a PR’s target branch changes (e.g., after a stacked parent merges and GitHub retargets the child)
Diffs
Stopped attributing GitHub PR comments authored by deleted (“ghost”) users to the wrong user in notifications
Documents
Fixed document mention notifications firing on every edit instead of just when a user is newly mentioned
Documents
Shortened the placeholder in the “Change document subscribers” action menu so its keyboard shortcut indicator no longer gets clipped
GitHub
Comments on a not-yet-linked GitHub PR now backfill the issue→PR link via magic-word matching the same way PR lifecycle and review events do
Inbox
Fixed “From is not” filter to correctly exclude issue assignment notifications from integration and OAuth application-driven assignments
Inbox
Fixed inbox badge getting stuck when arriving on a review via a legacy
/agent
URL
Insights
Fixed a bug where two Duplicate options appeared in the Time in status filter
Integrations

Figma
title instead of the raw marketing page title
Issues
Removed Duplicate status from quick issue create and issue template
Project templates
Allowed project-template sub-issues to target other private teams again
Sentry
Fixed a bug where Sentry-created issues could be assigned to deactivated users in triage
Settings
We now correctly truncate long team names in Teams settings list instead of overlapping the next column
Shortcuts

Sync

Triage

Views

Improvements
Android
Improved Android to use server-side filtering for skills
Agents
Dashboard pages are now used as context in the Linear agent chat
Integrations
Connecting GitHub now defaults to enabling code access
Project Slack channels
Used an info-style toast when starting project Slack channel creation
Reviews
Activity page file and code comment links, and PR notifications, now open whichever code view (Diffs or Guide) you last visited
Skills
Updated empty state for skills to match other settings
Timeline
Improved timeline projects aside panel visibility to be remembered per view instead of globally
Updates
Removed the UI for hiding project and initiative update details
May 21, 2026
Project Slack channels
Project teams often use a Slack channel to discuss and share feedback about their project as it takes shape.
Now, Linear can spin up that Slack channel automatically every time a new project is created. All project members are automatically added to the project Slack channel, and project updates post to that channel by default.
When working in a project’s Slack channel, you can use Linear Agent to answer questions or take actions in the project. Try sending messages to the channel like “
@Linear
what’s our target date?” or “
@Linear
file this bug and assign it to me.”
Admins can enable channel creation from
settings
. Learn more about project Slack channels in our
documentation
.
Linear Asks Agent
⁠
Linear Asks
brings internal requests into Linear, where the appropriate team can work on them.
Now, anyone in your Slack workspace can file a request in Linear by mentioning
@Linear Asks
. Linear checks the templates enabled for that channel, picks the best match, and creates an issue using the conversation as context.
When a template uses required fields, Linear follows up to collect any missing details before creating the issue.
Admins can configure Linear Asks agent in
settings
for any connected Slack workspace. Asks is available on Business and Enterprise plans. Learn more in our
documentation
.
Issue duplicates
⁠
Duplicate issues now use a dedicated status type in Linear and move context to the original issue.
When an issue is marked as a duplicate, its customer requests, synced Slack threads, and attachments are moved to the original issue. Whether a teammate, agent or Triage Intelligence marks an issue as a duplicate, the issue context moves to the right place.
Because of theses changes, every issue with a duplicate status must now link to the original issue. On duplicate issues, that original issue is shown clearly in the sidebar.
When the original issue is completed, every synced Slack thread is notified. Each linked customer ticket in Zendesk or Intercom also reopens, so you can keep your customers in the loop.
Reorder groups in issue and project views
⁠
Issues and projects in views are often organized into groups like status, priority, or label. You can now reorder these groups in any list or board view to put the most important sections first.
Click the reorder groups icon in display options, then drag groups into your preferred order. You can reset the view back to the default order at any time.
GitHub Enterprise Cloud integration
⁠
Linear now supports GitHub Enterprise Cloud.
Once connected, GitHub Enterprise Cloud users have access to all the benefits of our popular GitHub integration. Linked pull requests update issue statuses automatically, GitHub Issues sync can be enabled for select repos, and you can review PRs natively with Linear Diffs.
This integration is available on Linear’s Enterprise plan. Read more in our
documentation
, and install the
GitHub Enterprise Cloud integration
to get started.
Fixes

Filters

Filters

Filters

Labels
Restored input focus when switching between New group and New label while creating a label
Linear Agent

@Linear
, even after a long delay
Notifications
Fixed an issue where users could be unexpectedly subscribed to issues they never interacted with
Performance
Fixed an issue where switching workspaces could leave the app stuck on the workspace loading screen until a restart
Projects
Fixed public project Slack channels incorrectly appearing as private after renames
Releases
Fixed order of releases by stage position so canceled releases are sorted after completed ones
Reviews
Showed only the relative timestamp for opened pull requests in the review list
Settings
Fixed dropdown text overflowing on narrow viewports
Slack
Fixed double-notifying on Slack when an issue is deduplicated — the duplicate-relation event is now the only user-facing event
Sidebar
Reduced blur and fixed positioning in animated unread count digits on non-retina displays
Teams
Fixed issue where team owners can now update the “Restrict team membership” setting without needing workspace admin permissions
Templates
Fixed moving a project or document template to the parent team or workspace silently reverting
Updates
Switching the project or initiative on an update composer no longer deletes the previously saved draft
Workflow

API

Improvements

Issues

Cmd/CtrlK

MCP server

May 14, 2026
Code Intelligence

Code Intelligence gives Linear Agent controlled access to your codebase, turning repositories into shared product context your whole team can use.
With Code Intelligence, Linear Agent can reason about how your product actually works, not just what’s captured in issues, projects, and docs. Ask how a feature is implemented, why something behaves a certain way, what a change might affect, or which technical constraints should shape a plan or customer request without digging through the codebase or interrupting an engineer.
PMs can write sharper specs, Support and Sales can answer technical questions with more confidence, and Engineering can investigate bugs, regressions, and unfamiliar parts of the system faster.
To set up Code Intelligence, a workspace admin should:
Install the
GitHub integration
and enable code access
Turn on Code Intelligence in
AI Settings
From there, admins can choose which reposito

Travel
Bourdain’s been on my mind again. Not just the food, the knives, the back-of-the-kitchen grit — but how he moved through the world. Curious. Sharp. Honest. Flawed in a way that made sense. I miss that kind of honesty.
My Sourdough Bread
My Bread
My Sourdough Bread
500g Bread Flour
350g Lukewarm Water
100g Active Sourdough Starter
10g Of Salt
Start in the morning. Grab a big bowl and toss in 500g of bread flour (or all-purpose, don’t overthink it) with 350g of lukewarm water. Stir it into a shaggy mess. You’re not kneading, just mixing. Cover the bowl and walk away for 30–45 minutes. Let the flour and water get to know each other, this is the autolyse, where the magic begins.
Come back. Now, add 100g of your bubbly sourdough starter (you fed it last night, right?) and 10g of salt. Use your hands and mix it all together. It’ll be sticky, borderline annoying, but just keep folding and squishing until it feels more like a dough than a swamp.

changelog.title: What's new
changelog.text:
What's new
What's new
;
What's new
Hi there,
Today we are sharing two big updates - quality-of-life upgrades to
Collections
in the native apps, and a major
under-the-hood rework
on Web and Windows. No new features this time, both releases focus on making Craft better to use every day, and both are just the first step of a broader push to
improve the overall quality
. We'd love to hear which quality improvements you'd like to see next!
Plus: 2 days left on our
Spring Sale
(40% off Plus and Family, for life), and if you're in
San Francisco the week of June 8
, save the date for our
first ever community meetup
.
Spring Sale - 2 days left!
Until
end of Thursday, May 21
, get a
40% recurring discount
on all new
Plus and Family subscriptions
, including upgrades from older Pro and Business plans. Available only on the
web app
.
Use
SPRING26
during checkout for a
40% lifetime discount
❓️FAQ on the Spring Sale
Can I use this discount for existing subscriptions?
Only if you upgrade, for example from the old Pro Tier to Plus.
If you run into any issues during the subscription or upgrade flow, just email us at
support@craft.do
!
Can I use this discount when subscribing via the native apps?
No, you’ll need to log in on the
Web app
to subscribe or upgrade there.
Collections
This update is mainly focusing on collections to iron out usability issues and expand editing capabilities in-line on the smallest devices. We are not introducing anything new rather aiming to improve the overall experience.
Kanban - built for flow
Add items, anywhere, fast
•
New always-visible "+" button at the bottom of every column
•
Hover top of column on macOS for quick add & options (always visible on iOS/iPadOS)
•
Top toolbar "+" adds to the first column in one click
Smarter item interactions
•
Click to select on macOS / iPadOS+mouse → edit title and fields inline, delete, copy/paste
•
Tap on iOS/iPadOS jumps straight to the subpage
•
New button to enter subpages on hover
Right-click / long-press menu
•
New
Edit fields
action: see all properties, edit values, or add a new field — without leaving the board
Groups, finally inline
•
Add a new group with a single click — hover handle on macOS, pseudo-column on touch
•
Edit, hide, or delete groups from the new top 3-dot menu
Gallery - cleaner adding, same shortcuts
•
New always-visible "+" tile to add the next item — smartly resizes to fit even/odd grids
•
macOS: select the collection to reveal the circular blue add button
•
Same click / right-click / multi-select behavior as Kanban — one mental model across views
Table - quick edits, quick adds
•
Subtle blue dots on macOS to add items and
new fields
inline
•
Full-screen and touch: empty row at the bottom for fast entry, plus "+" column on the right for new fields
•
Tap a cell to select, tap again to edit. Tap the title cell → right arrow button to open the subpage
•
Tap the row number column on iOS/iPadOS for instant multi-select
Redesigned View & Collection options
We combined the old "Kanban/Gallery/Table View" and "Change view" menus into a single panel with a tab bar on top — switch layout, tweak visibility, and manage views all in one place.
•
macOS:
click an active view to open the focused list
•
macOS & iPadOS:
tap the slider icon in the top-right toolbar.
•
iOS:
tap the view dropdown to open the full context menu — view options live under
Edit view
, with collection actions right below.
Other improvements on the native apps
•
Significant
performance improvements
for
large tables.
•
Type
@
in a document title to insert links
- same as in text blocks. Mention people, link to other documents, or reference tasks directly from the title.
•
Cmd+Option+T
now
reliably closes all toggles
when you've selected toggles together with their content (for example, after Cmd+A).
•
Tap a custom reminder
to
jump
straight
into editing
•
Renamed the find-and-replace option
"Ignore Diacritics"
to "Diacritics Insensitive"
for consistency with the other, "Case Insensitive" option.
Web & Windows major under the hood rework
We've been working hard to significantly improve Craft on Web and Windows. This release is the first step in a broader push to raise the overall quality of Craft across every platform, expect more to follow.
It brings personalization, performance, and polish: new tabs open faster, memory usage is smarter, a new in-app update banner keeps you current, and we've fixed a long list of keyboard, sync, and editor issues, including a major fix for Dvorak, QWERTZ, and AZERTY layouts.
Highlights
•
Faster, smoother app:
New tabs open noticeably quicker thanks to a pre-warmed view. Idle background tabs are auto-released after 10 minutes to keep memory in check.
•
In-app updates:
A new update banner appears when an update is ready, plus a manual Check for updates button in the About section.
•
Daily-note date navigator:
A new pill control lets you jump between days quickly.
•
Quicker navigation:
"Open in new tab" is now available in folder and shared-with-me menus, and every document card has a handy Copy link action.
•
Cross-tab sync:
Language, theme, and "What's New" dismissal now stay in sync across all your open tabs.
•
Big keyboard fix:
Letter shortcuts and Cmd+. / Cmd+/ now work correctly on Dvorak, QWERTZ, AZERTY, and other non-US keyboard layouts.
•
Personalize your accent color:
Pick any accent color and watch it flow through the editor: links, selection, drag highlights, the daily-note "Today" header, and icon badges all adapt automatically. Dark mode keeps everything readable, even on bright accents.
Editor improvements
•
Pasting into a table cell now lands inside the cell instead of below the table
•
Single-click no longer toggles edit/select on empty table cells
•
Improved formula editing in tables
•
New visible-section indicator in the Table of Contents sidebar
•
Smoother sidebar animations and tighter row spacing
•
Fixed cover image clashing with content in wide page layout
•
Select block on right-click
Collections
•
Fixed SUM aggregation precision and grouped-calculation scope
•
Kanban: fixed infinite render loop in group toggles and card insertion with duplicated column names
•
Faster gallery card opening on fast double-click
•
Better theming in popups and menus
Sync & stability
•
More reliable cross-tab logout and restored Windows context menu
•
Fixed clipping on maximized windows
•
Folder move-to-root sync and root folder ordering fixed
•
Tab state survives disk errors gracefully, no more crashes on low disk space
•
Fewer memory leaks and render loops throughout the editor

Introduction
Craft Documentation
Learn how to use Craft – the powerful, beautiful tool for creating documents, managing tasks, and organizing your work and life.
Documentation Index
Fetch the complete documentation index at:
https://craft-support.mintlify.app/llms.txt
Use this file to discover all available pages before exploring further.
Welcome to Craft! Whether you’re new to the app or looking to master advanced features, this documentation will help you get the most out of your experience.
Craft is more than a note-taking app – it’s a complete workspace for thinking, creating, planning, and collaborating. From AI-powered assistance to powerful organization tools, Craft adapts to how you work.
​
Getting started
New to Craft? Start here to learn the fundamentals and set up your workspace.
Introduction
Get started with Craft and learn the basics of documents, navigation, and essential features
Move to Craft
Migrate your notes from Notion, Obsidian, Evernote, and other platforms
​
Video Tutorials
New to Craft? Watch our Craft 101 intro to get started in just one minute.
Watch the full Craft 101 series →
11 video tutorials covering everything from basics to advanced features
​
Core features
Master the essential capabilities that make Craft powerful.
Write and Edit
Create beautiful documents with formatting, styling, media, and advanced editing tools
Plan and Do
Stay organized with tasks, calendar, daily notes, and reminders all in one place
Organize and Find
Use collections, linking, search, spaces, and tags to keep everything organized

analysis.name: Craft
analysis.tagline: Your space for notes, tasks, and big ideas
analysis.value_proposition: An all-in-one workspace for writing, tasks, calendar, and whiteboards that replaces multiple apps at a fraction of the cost.
analysis.ideal_customer: Individual creators, students, and small teams who want a beautiful, unified workspace for notes, tasks, and docs across Apple and Windows devices

differentiators: All-in-one workspace (notes, tasks, calendar, whiteboards) replacing multiple apps at $8/month vs $46/month for equivalents
Fixed-price family and team plans (not per-seat scaling) — Family is flat $15 for up to 6, Team flat $50 for up to 10
Native MCP server connections to AI coding tools (Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, Raycast)
Native Apple Vision Pro app
Apple Design Awards finalist and Mac App of the Year winner — design-first positioning
Block-based content model enabling structured databases (Collections) alongside freeform docs in one surface
Offline editing on Web, Windows, and Android

recent_ships.what: Collections overhaul: Kanban always-visible add buttons, inline group editing, unified view/options panel; Gallery smart resizing; Table inline field add and touch multi-select
recent_ships.source: Collections - built for flow: New always-visible '+' button at the bottom of every column

recent_ships.what: Web & Windows major under-the-hood rework: pre-warmed tab loading, idle tab memory release, in-app update banner, daily-note date navigator, accent color personalization, Dvorak/QWERTZ/AZERTY keyboard fix, cross-tab sync
recent_ships.source: We've been working hard to significantly improve Craft on Web and Windows. This release is the first step in a broader push

recent_ships.what: @ mentions in document titles for linking people, documents, and tasks
recent_ships.source: Type @ in a document title to insert links - same as in text blocks.

recent_ships.what: MCP and API expansion: full space access, daily notes, tasks, and advanced search via API/MCP
recent_ships.source: MCP and API expansion – Full space access, daily notes, tasks, and advanced search capabilities

recent_ships.what: AI Assistant: Image and PDF OCR, custom prompts with search, Help Agent integration
recent_ships.source: AI Assistant enhancements – Image and PDF OCR, custom prompts with search, Help Agent integration

recent_ships.what: Offline editing on Web, Windows, and Android
recent_ships.source: Cross-platform updates – Space-level Assistant and offline editing on Web, Windows, and Android

praised: Publishing directly from Craft just feels natural — it's where I write, so it makes sense to share from there too. (Tom, podcaster)

free_tier.limits: 1500 blocks total, 1 GB storage, 25 MB media upload, 7-day version history, 15 AI credits, no shared space

tiers.name: Starter
tiers.headline_inclusions: All features with 1500-block content limit, 1 GB storage, 15 AI credits, Core+Fast AI models

tiers.name: Plus
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited blocks/storage/media, 30-day history, 50 AI credits/mo, Core+Fast+Max models, shared space

tiers.name: Family
tiers.headline_inclusions: 2–6 Plus accounts in one flat subscription with shared Space

tiers.name: Team
tiers.headline_inclusions: Up to 10 Plus accounts in one flat subscription with shared Space

pricing.notes: Prices shown are yearly billing rates; monthly billing available at higher rates (exact monthly prices not captured in source). Spring 2026 sale offered 40% lifetime discount (code SPRING26) on Plus and Family for new subscriptions via web app only. Groups larger than 10 contact sales. Education plan exists but pricing not captured. Regional pricing variations noted in FAQ.

The OS for

teams
Supercharge your teams to get things done
Get started
View demo
85% of Fortune 100 companies choose Asana¹
AI that works the way your team works
Asana Work Graph®
A neural network of everything your company is doing, every person, task, project, goal, and dependency connected, so humans and agents always know who is doing what, by when, and toward which goal.
Multiplayer
Every workflow, app, and agent runs in a shared space where humans and agents act on the same plan, see the same context, and get smarter with every interaction. Multiple humans can train, guide, and improve any agent.
Shared memory
Shared memory means AI Teammates learn from the work itself. Every completed task, every piece of feedback, every preference gets carried forward, so no one repeats context they've already given and every workflow starts smarter than the last.
Enterprise governance
Every agent has an identity, scoped permissions, an audit trail, and cost constraints, with data access, approved actions, and spend all managed from the same console that governs human users.
AI Teammates
Your team just got bigger
Pre-built AI agents that work alongside your team inside real workflows, with shared memory, governance, and context from the Work Graph, so they are ready to go on day one.

Privacy Preference Center
When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. However, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.
Allow All
Manage Consent Preferences
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are strictly necessary to provide you with certain features. For example, these cookies allow you to access secure areas that require registration and set your privacy preferences. Because these cookies are essential to providing services to you, they cannot be disabled. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but it may cause some parts of the site to not work.
Cookies Details
Performance Cookies
Performance Cookies
These cookies allow us or our third-party analytics providers to collect information and statistics on use of our services by you and other visitors. This information helps us to improve our services and products for the benefit of you and others.
Cookies Details
Functional Cookies
Functional Cookies
These cookies provide enhanced functionality, providing chat support, allowing you to more easily complete forms, personalizing content to your preferences, and selecting your communications preferences. If you do not enable these cookies, or choose to disable them in the future, that could impact your ability to use certain features.
Cookies Details
Targeting Cookies
Targeting Cookies
These cookies, provided by our third-party advertising partners, collect information about your browsing habits, as well as your preferences for various features and services. They also provide us with auditing, research, and reporting to know when advertising content has been displayed and how successful the content has been. This information allows us and our third-party advertising providers to display relevant advertising content.

Privacy Preference Center
When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. However, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.
Allow All
Manage Consent Preferences
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are strictly necessary to provide you with certain features. For example, these cookies allow you to access secure areas that require registration and set your privacy preferences. Because these cookies are essential to providing services to you, they cannot be disabled. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but it may cause some parts of the site to not work.
Cookies Details
Performance Cookies
Performance Cookies
These cookies allow us or our third-party analytics providers to collect information and statistics on use of our services by you and other visitors. This information helps us to improve our services and products for the benefit of you and others.
Cookies Details
Functional Cookies
Functional Cookies
These cookies provide enhanced functionality, providing chat support, allowing you to more easily complete forms, personalizing content to your preferences, and selecting your communications preferences. If you do not enable these cookies, or choose to disable them in the future, that could impact your ability to use certain features.
Cookies Details
Targeting Cookies
Targeting Cookies
These cookies, provided by our third-party advertising partners, collect information about your browsing habits, as well as your preferences for various features and services. They also provide us with auditing, research, and reporting to know when advertising content has been displayed and how successful the content has been. This information allows us and our third-party advertising providers to display relevant advertising content.
Cookies Details

tiers.name: Personal
tiers.headline_inclusions: 2 users, unlimited tasks/projects, list/board/calendar views, 100+ integrations

tiers.name: Starter
tiers.headline_inclusions: Timeline/Gantt, dashboards, unlimited automations, forms, AI Studio 50K credits

tiers.name: Advanced
tiers.headline_inclusions: Portfolios, goals, workload, AI Studio 75K credits

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: Advanced security, admin controls, enterprise governance — contact sales

tiers.name: Enterprise+
tiers.headline_inclusions: Highest tier with advanced compliance — contact sales

pricing.notes: Yearly billing saves up to 18%; AI Studio credits scale per tier (50K Starter, 75K Advanced); billed per user with no seat cap on paid tiers

FAQ
1. What are the different types of licenses?
Wrike has a variety of different options available to suit your team, including two types of user licenses: full users and limited users.
Full users have more default permissions enabled and can do more in Wrike. Regular users and external users are full users and require a paid-user seat (note that external users are limited to certain access rights).
Limited users have fewer permissions and limited access to certain Wrike features. Limited users require a paid user seat at a reduced rate to that of a full user license (as contributors) or may be provisioned into the workspace for free (as viewers).
Read more
2. What happens when my trial has expired?
Your account will be blocked until you choose what you’d like to do with it. From here, you can either downgrade it to the free version or reach out to your Wrike representative. They will be able to discuss the next steps for choosing a plan and whether a trial extension is possible. While your account is blocked, your data will remain intact.
Read more
3. How do I purchase a subscription?
If you are a first-time user, enter your business email at the top of the page to get your 14-day free trial. You can give us a call to get set up straight away or wait for one of our representatives to get in touch to find the right solution for your team.
If you’ve completed a trial, you can purchase the Team plan and the Business plan for up to 15 users with no add-ons in the Account section of your workspace. If you need a larger number of users or a wider set of capabilities, you’ll need to contact your Wrike representative.
Wrike is sold in groups of users. For accounts of up to 30 seats, subscriptions are sold in groups of five. For accounts of 30 to 100 seats, subscriptions are sold in groups of 10. For accounts above 100 seats, subscriptions are sold in groups of 25. Wrike Business plans and above are only available as annual subscriptions.
Read more
4. Can I pay in my local currency and what kind of taxes apply?
All our prices are listed in US dollars, but your bank will be able to convert this to your local currency upon purchase. You will also receive your invoice in US dollars. In regards to tax implications, all of our prices do not include applicable state/local taxes and you (the client) will be responsible for them. If you have any questions, please speak to your sales representative and we’ll be happy to help.
5. Where will my data be hosted?
The location of your data is automatically determined by the geographic region where you register. Accounts created from Europe are hosted in our European data center in Paris, France, while all other accounts are hosted in our primary U.S. data center in Santa Clara, California. If you need to change your data center location, please contact your Wrike representative or our support team.
Learn more
6. Does Wrike have an on-premise solution or is it just cloud-based?
Wrike is entirely cloud-based, meaning there is no need for site visits and our software can be used instantly by remote workers.
7. What does Wrike do to ensure GDPR compliance?
Wrike takes data privacy/security very seriously. We have the following independent third-party certifications:
SOC2 Type II, which shows our commitment to taking a robust and secure approach to products, processes, and security surrounding our customer data
ISO 27001:2013, which confirms that Wrike has an end-to-end security framework and a risk-based approach to managing information security
ISO 27018:2019, which shows Wrike has controls in place to protect Personally Identifiable Information (PII) in accordance with the privacy principles in ISO/IEC 29100 for the public cloud computing environment
CSA STAR, which demonstrates that Wrike has addressed issues critical to cloud security as outlined in the CSA Cloud Controls Matrix, and has been assessed against the STAR Capability Maturity Model for activities in cloud security control areas
In addition to the above certifications, we are
EU & US and Swiss & US Privacy Shield-certified
, GDPR-compliant and CCPA-compliant. We also have an
EU data center
that can be selected to use for our services.
8. What is a Professional Services deployment add-on?
Professional Services refers to the consultancy service that we offer (see the question below). Wrike offers a selection of Professional Services packages designed to successfully onboard your team. Our consultancy team will make sure you get the most from the Wrike solution that you have selected. To learn more about Wrike’s consultancy options, please see our
Professional Services page
.
9. From what team size do you recommend adding a Professional Services deployment to my solution?
Wrike is an intuitive collaboration solution. However, aligning your entire team on a consistent way of working, configuring Wrike, and ensuring long-term adoption can be tricky. Our Professional Services team specializes in understanding and onboarding teams of all sizes, no matter their challenges. We recommend all new teams work with a consultant during onboarding, and insist that teams of 20+ utilize a guided deployment.
10.
Do I need to pay extra to have access to Wrike Copilot and AI agents?
No. All plans starting from the Business tier include a number of AI Elite actions. Entry tiers provide a starter package for experimentation, while the highest tier offers generous action volume to run end-to-end workflows.
In addition to your plan, Wrike also offers an add-on to increase the number of AI Elite actions for your account.
Contact us
to know more about Wrike AI solutions.
11.
If I exceed my plan’s AI action limit, will I be invoiced automatically?
No. Wrike is known for its transparent and fair pricing. This means that when a customer goes beyond their plan limit, a discussion is opened around usage and the best package to power them.

spec.body:
# ZergStack

**Current rule, updated from Idan Slack feedback on 2026-05-14:** ZergStack / zstack is the infrastructure and runtime stack, not the umbrella brand for user-facing products.

Public brand name is **ZergStack** when referring to the infrastructure product or runtime layer. Lowercase `zstack` is reserved for URLs, filesystem paths, config, and internal shorthand. Older copy that uses "ZergStack" as the product-family umbrella should be treated as stale until rewritten.

Source of truth for managed apps: `~/zerg/zstack.yaml`. Internal control plane: `~/zerg/zstack/` (a mini Render/Railway that deploys to Fly.io and surfaces live status). The public framing is close to Render/Vercel style infrastructure: code push -> deploy, runtime, app hosting, service status, and shared primitives.

## Zerg Products

Default temporary umbrella language for user-facing products is **Zerg products**. Use this until Matt/Idan choose a final family term. Do not use "marketplace" as the default umbrella; it implies third-party contributors and payments before that model is decided.

Current user-facing products / product concepts:

- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zergboard/Zergboard|Zergboard]] — project / task management (Trello/Linear-style) · competitive: [[Competitive/pm-software/index|pm-software]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergCRM/ZergCRM|ZergCRM]] — internal-only, on roadmap, no early-access flow
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergChat/ZergChat|ZergChat]] — Slack-style team messaging · competitive: [[Competitive/internal-chat/index|internal-chat]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergCal/ZergCal|ZergCal]] — calendar service (humans, interop, agent-native) · competitive: [[Competitive/calendar/index|calendar]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergMeeting/ZergMeeting|ZergMeeting]] — meeting capture / notes / actioning · competitive: [[Competitive/video-meetings/index|video-meetings]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergMail/ZergMail|ZergMail]] — agent-friendly email server · competitive: [[Competitive/workspace-email/index|workspace-email]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zergwallet/Zergwallet|Zergwallet]] — wallet for zergs to hold/spend USD/BTC/ETH · competitive: [[Competitive/finance-and-wallets/index|finance-and-wallets]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zergaudience/zergaudience-audience-taxonomy|Zergaudience]] — inbound capture / contacts / segments layer

Integration narrative: ZergMeeting → Zergboard → ZergChat → ZergCal → ZergMail. Full mapping (data flow, technical foundations, shipped-vs-spec'd status) lives in [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/Integration|Integration Narrative]].

Cross-product competitive sweeps should now say "Zerg products" unless the claim is specifically about the ZergStack infra layer.

## Apps Managed by ZergStack

These may be deployed via `zstack.yaml`, but that does not automatically make them part of a public product-family brand:

- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergBox/ZergBox|ZergBox]] — file/upload utility app
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZMsg/ZMsg|ZMsg]] — provider-aware messaging gateway (SMS/MMS/WhatsApp via Telnyx, Twilio)

The engineering-platform layer (`Zerg`, `ZDE`, `ZTC`, `Zerg Cloud`, `zterm`) is separate scope from both public Zerg products and the ZergStack infra page. Current internal evidence and open reliability/onboarding findings live in [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/ZTC|ZTC]]; zterm product/backlog direction lives in [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zterm/Zterm|Zterm]].

## Brand & Naming Conventions

- Public/long names: `Zergboard`, `ZergCRM`, `ZergChat`, `ZergCal`, `ZergMeeting`, `ZergMail`, `Zergwallet`, `Zergaudience`, `ZergStack`
- Short names reserved for internal/dev: `zboard`, `zchat`, `zcal`, `zmail`, `zcrm`
- One domain per product (registration tracked under WEB-30 on the Website Zergboard board)
- Product pages can say "runs on ZergStack" when discussing infra/runtime. They should not use "part of ZergStack" as the primary product positioning.
- Green-heavy product pages are not the default visual direction for Zergboard/Zergwallet. Per Idan 2026-05-14, use the more subdued Zergaudience-style dark treatment when using the green palette.

## Pricing

**Locked 2026-05-05** (vampire-attack on individual SaaS competitors):

| Tier | Price | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Free | $0 | Volume-capped per product |
| Basic | **$1/seat/mo** | Full feature surface; no agent governance |
| Pro | **$9/seat/mo** | + Scoped per-agent API tokens, tenant-safe routes, SSO, audit, advanced roles |
| Enterprise | Custom | + SLA, BYO-KMS, on-prem, SOC 2 / DPA |

Bundle SKUs remain proposed and should be renamed away from "Zstack" in public copy unless Matt/Idan explicitly choose that terminology. Working internal examples: **$4/seat Zerg products Basic** (20% off 5x Basic) and **$19/seat Zerg products Pro** (58% off 5x Pro). Strategy memory: `project_zstack_pricing.md`.

Per-product feature splits: [[Writing/Zstack Microproduct Pricing (WEB-31)]]. Category-leader benchmarks: [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/Pricing-Snapshot|Pricing Snapshot]] (now updated with locked decisions). Implementation rolled out for Zergboard + zmail in PR #283.

## Auth & Security

- Auth unification deferred until product-family bundle launch (WEB-10); provider pre-work under WEB-38
- Security page lives at zergai.com/security; MNDA-gated request flow (no public PDFs)
- SOC 2 Type II target placeholder Q3 2026 (WEB-37)
- DPA / sub-processor list / MNDA template under WEB-34, WEB-35, WEB-36

## Bootstrap Recipe (New Microproduct)

From [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zergwallet/Zergwallet|Zergwallet]]. The recipe generalizes to any new Zerg product:

1. `cd ~/zerg && mkdir <appname> && cd <appname>`
2. `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions`
3. `/rename <appname>`
4. Paste a bootstrap prompt referencing `~/zerg/zergboard` and `~/zerg/zchat` as patterns to mimic (both use the ZTC embedded chat agent)
5. Use a branch like `zstack/<appname>` to keep work isolated

## Related

- [[Epoch/Projects/Zergboard Snapshot|Zergboard Snapshot]] — board-level pull from the zergboard-skill
- [[Epoch/Projects/Product Glossary|Product Glossary]] — context on Zerg, ZDE, ZTC, ZStack as engineering layer
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/ZTC|ZTC]] — internal engineering-platform source of truth and Slack-sourced evidence capture

positioning.elevator_pitch: ZergStack is an agent-native infrastructure and runtime layer for teams building integrated products — code push to deploy, with shared primitives across projects, chat, calendar, mail, and wallet — for founders who want tightly-integrated Zerg products instead of stitching Notion/Airtable/ClickUp together.
core_differentiator_lines: Agent-native from day one, not bolted on
Integrated Zerg products share one runtime, one identity
You own the data — no Notion-style export friction
Render/Vercel-style deploys with product primitives built in
Managed apps deploy from one zstack.yaml, not five dashboards

headline_candidates.headline: The runtime built for agents, not just humans
headline_candidates.subhead: Ship Zerg products on shared infra — chat, calendar, mail, wallet — without gluing five SaaS tools together.

headline_candidates.headline: Code push. Deploy. Done.
headline_candidates.subhead: Render/Vercel-style app hosting with product primitives baked in — no Notion lock-in, no ClickUp bloat.

headline_candidates.headline: Your data. Your runtime. Your stack.
headline_candidates.subhead: ZergStack runs your integrated products on Fly.io with a live control plane and shared identity across every app.

headline_candidates.headline: One yaml. Every app.
headline_candidates.subhead: zstack.yaml is the source of truth. Zergboard, ZergChat, ZergCal, ZergMail, Zergwallet — one deploy, one runtime, one identity.

headline_candidates.headline: Stop stitching Notion to Airtable to ClickUp
headline_candidates.subhead: ZergStack ships the integrated primitives — tasks, docs, chat, calendar, mail — as one runtime you control.

messaging_pillars.promise: Every primitive is designed for agents and humans equally — not retrofitted
proof_points: ZergMail is an agent-friendly email server, not a human-only inbox
ZergCal is explicitly humans + interop + agent-native
Shared identity means agents move across chat, calendar, mail, and tasks natively

messaging_pillars.promise: The five products share one runtime, one identity, one deploy — no glue code
proof_points: ZergMeeting → Zergboard → ZergChat → ZergCal → ZergMail data flow is native, not via Zapier
One zstack.yaml deploys the whole stack
Live control plane surfaces status across every managed app

messaging_pillars.promise: Your data, your runtime, your control plane — no vendor lock-in
proof_points: Deploys to Fly.io that you control
No Notion-style export friction — data lives in your runtime
Render/Vercel-style workflow means you can leave whenever

messaging_pillars.promise: Not just hosting — the shared primitives (identity, wallet, calendar, mail) come with the runtime
proof_points: Zergwallet ships USD/BTC/ETH holding as a first-class primitive
Zstack.yaml declares apps AND their shared primitives

anti_pitch: We are NOT an all-in-one doc/database like Notion or Coda — those become learning-curve nightmares (Coda HN: 'steep learning curve for doc-plus-app hybrid'; Fibery HN: 'powerful for those who can handle it')
We are NOT a bloated PM tool like ClickUp — users literally build their own alternatives after trying it (HN: 'I built my own PM tool after trying Trello, Asana, ClickUp, etc.' — 632 upvotes)
We are NOT a marketplace — do not imply third-party contributors or payments before that model is decided (per 2026-05-14 Idan rule)
We are NOT closed-data infra — Notion's data lock-in complaints ('withholding my company data') are the exact pattern to avoid
We are NOT a Grammarly-style acquisition risk — Coda's redirect to Superhuman shows what happens when infra vendors get absorbed
positioning.icp_refinement: Founders and small technical teams building integrated multi-product companies (agents + humans) who currently glue Notion + Airtable + ClickUp + Slack + Google Workspace together and hate it — specifically technical operators who want Render/Vercel-style deploy ergonomics AND product primitives (chat, cal, mail, wallet) as one runtime rather than five vendors.

objection_handling.objection: Notion/ClickUp/Airtable is cheaper and more mature
objection_handling.response: Maturity in this category means lock-in (Notion export friction), bloat (ClickUp's 22% layoff signals cost pressure passed to customers), or acquisition risk (Coda→Grammarly→Superhuman). ZergStack ships the primitives you actually need, on infra you control, without the tax.

objection_handling.objection: Why not just use Render/Vercel + best-of-breed SaaS?
objection_handling.response: Because gluing five vendors together is the problem — identity, data flow, and agent-access break at every seam. ZergStack ships the shared runtime AND the primitives (chat, cal, mail, wallet) so integration is native, not a Zapier tax.

objection_handling.objection: You're missing table-stakes features (kanban, calendar view, automations, MCP, native integrations)
objection_handling.response: Correct — we're closing table-stakes gaps in Q3/Q4. But the differentiator isn't feature parity; it's that our primitives are agent-native and share one runtime. Feature-parity competitors can't retrofit that.

objection_handling.objection: Fly.io is a single point of failure
objection_handling.response: Fly.io is the current deploy target, not the architecture. The control plane and zstack.yaml abstraction mean the runtime is portable — and unlike Notion/Coda, your data isn't hostage to the vendor.

objection_handling.objection: This looks like another all-in-one that becomes a mess (à la ClickUp/Fibery)
objection_handling.response: ZergStack is explicitly infra + primitives, not a monolithic app. Each Zerg product (Zergboard, ZergChat, ZergCal, ZergMail, Zergwallet) is a focused, standalone service — the integration happens at the runtime layer, not by cramming features into one UI.

objection_handling.objection: Reliability — Fibery had a 2.5-hour outage post-mortem, why are you different?
objection_handling.response: We ship a live control plane that surfaces service status in-product, and the runtime is code-push-to-deploy so you can roll back fast. We're not claiming zero downtime; we're claiming observable, portable infra.
